alignments.preprint_claim_text: Citizen scientists can perform relationship extraction in biomedical texts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Citizen scientists can perform relationship extraction in biomedical texts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result verbatim, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate named entity recognition is important for the task of relationship extraction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate named entity recognition is important for user performance in relationship extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the target (specifying 'user performance') but keeps the same core claim about the importance of accurate NER.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The process of generating gold standards for information extraction is time consuming and could benefit from citizen science. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data generated by citizen scientists can identify relationship types not currently available in the Mark2Cure Relationship Extraction Module. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts focus from a general claim about saving time/gold-standard generation to a more specific capability (identifying relationship types), representing a change in emphasis and narrowing of scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Citizen scientists have previously been found to be capable of performing named entity recognition of disease mentions in biomedical abstracts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication statement corresponds to this prior-result claim; instead the paper emphasizes complementary data sources, so the explicit NER-capability claim is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Citizen science-generated data and algorithm-mined data can complement each other in biomedical information extraction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about complementarity does not have an analogue in the preprint and appears as a new integrative claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed computational framework can classify regulatory regions based on sequence features with an accuracy above 90%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a computational framework to classify regulatory regions based on sequence features with an average accuracy above 90%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim (above 90% average accuracy) with consistent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 'bag-of-k-mers' model achieved higher overall accuracy compared to the 'vector-k-mers' model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 'bag-of-k-mers' model achieved higher overall accuracy compared to the 'vector-k-mers' model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about relative model performance is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 'vector-k-mers' models are more useful in highlighting key groups of sequences within the regulatory regions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 'vector-k-mers' models were more useful in highlighting key groups of sequences within the regulatory regions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same observation about the vector-k-mers models' utility; wording differences are minor and do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models provide powerful tools to annotate regulatory regions in other maize lines beyond the reference. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These models provide powerful tools to annotate regulatory regions in other maize lines beyond the reference. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried into the publication with equivalent scope and certainty; only the label shifts from contribution to conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioEmu can generate thousands of statistically independent samples from the protein structure ensemble per hour on a single graphics processing unit. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BioEmu is a deep learning system that emulates protein equilibrium ensembles by generating thousands of statistically independent structures per hour on a single GPU. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that BioEmu is a deep learning system and explicitly frames the generation as emulation of equilibrium ensembles, clarifying method details while keeping the throughput claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioEmu's protein ensembles represent equilibrium in a range of challenging and practically relevant metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BioEmu captures diverse functional motions, including cryptic pocket formation, local unfolding, and domain rearrangements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The focus in the publication shifts from equilibrium-representation metrics to capturing diverse functional motions; this represents a substantive change in emphasis and a broader characterization of capabilities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioEmu samples protein conformations with relative free energy errors around 1 kcal/mol, as validated against millisecond-timescale MD simulation and experimentally-measured protein stabilities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BioEmu predicts relative free energies with 1 kilocalorie per mole accuracy compared with millisecond-scale MD and experimental data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same quantitative free-energy accuracy claim and the same validation comparisons.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioEmu reveals mechanistic insights, such as the causes for fold destabilization of mutants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BioEmu provides mechanistic insights by jointly modeling structural ensembles and thermodynamic properties. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the mechanistic claim and attributes the insight to joint modeling of ensembles and thermodynamics, broadening and clarifying the mechanism relative to the specific example in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BioEmu demonstrates a scalable path toward understanding and designing protein function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader conclusion about a scalable path to design/understanding protein function is not present in the preprint and appears as a new high-level claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GOBoost outperformed state-of-the-art methods across all evaluation metrics on both the PDB and AF2 datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GOBoost outperformed state-of-the-art methods on all evaluation metrics for both the PDB and AF2 datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance claim across datasets and metrics with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the AUPR evaluation on the PDB test set, GOBoost improved by 10.71%, 35.91%, and 22.71% compared to the SOTA HEAL method in the MF, BP, and CC functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the AUPR evaluation on the PDB test set, GOBoost improved by 10.71%, 35.91%, and 22.71% compared to the SOTA HEAL method in the MF, BP, and CC functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The detailed numeric improvements are preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate the necessity and superiority of designing models from the label long-tail distribution perspective. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results show the necessity and superiority of designing models from the label long-tail distribution perspective. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The wording of the conclusion is effectively the same, but the publication reports a lower (medium) certainty, indicating a tempered confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAsmol demonstrates accurate predictions of the binding between RNA and small molecules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAsmol demonstrates accurate predictions of the binding between RNA and small molecules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The central performance claim is preserved verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAsmol outperforms other methods with average improvements of ∼8% (AUROC) in 10-fold cross-validation, ∼16% (AUROC) in cold evaluation, and ∼30% (ranking score) in decoy evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAsmol outperforms other methods in ten-fold cross-validation, unseen evaluation, and decoy evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the outperforming claim across the same evaluation types but omits the numerical improvements; this generalizes and slightly abstracts the original quantified claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAsmol can generate reliable predictions without requiring structural input. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAsmol can generate reliable predictions without requiring structural input. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same in content but the publication reports it with lower certainty (medium), indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's interpretability is validated through case studies that identify molecular binding hotspots. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the specific claim about interpretability case studies and binding-hotspot validation, so the specific result appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAsmol incorporates data perturbation with augmentation, graph-based molecular feature representation, and attention-based feature fusion modules. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This detailed methodological description of model components is not present in the preprint claims list and appears as a new contribution claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models developed in this study accurately predict treatment outcomes for sertraline, bupropion, and placebo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model for sertraline achieved R² of 48% in predicting the change in Hamilton Depression Rating Scale. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a precise quantitative result for sertraline whereas the preprint made a general accuracy claim across three treatments; this clarifies and narrows the original broad claim to sertraline-specific performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models explain up to 37% of the variance in ΔHAMD and classify remitters with a number needed to treat (NNT) of 2.3-4.3. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model for sertraline achieved R² of 48% in predicting the change in Hamilton Depression Rating Scale. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different (higher) R² for sertraline and omits the NNT statistic, so the quantitative claim about explained variance is revised (amplified for sertraline) and the scope is narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New regions predictive of treatment outcome, such as the hippocampus and paracentral lobule, were identified. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Brain regions where reward processing activity was predictive included the prefrontal cortex and cerebellar crus 1 for sertraline and the cingulate cortex, caudate, orbitofrontal cortex, and crus 1 for bupropion. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports predictive regions, but the specific regions listed differ from those named in the preprint, so the claim content is revised while maintaining the same general scope (identifying predictive regions).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct models for each treatment provide substantial evidence of their potential to improve precision in treatment selection for major depressive disorder. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings demonstrate the utility of reward processing measurements and deep learning to predict antidepressant outcomes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same overall conclusion as demonstrating utility of the methods for predicting antidepressant outcomes, which clarifies and slightly reframes the preprint's claim without changing its scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MuRaL achieves better predictive performance than current state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MuRaL achieves better predictive performance than current state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim verbatim/with same emphasis as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MuRaL can build models with relatively few training mutations and a moderate number of sequenced individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MuRaL can build models with relatively few training mutations and a moderate number of sequenced individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about data-efficiency is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MuRaL can leverage transfer learning to further reduce data and time demands. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MuRaL can leverage transfer learning to further reduce data and time demands. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the transfer learning advantage claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of MuRaL produces genome-wide mutation rate maps for four representative species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the explicit statement that genome-wide maps were produced for four species, so this preprint claim appears omitted from the publication's claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improved mutation rate estimates allow for the stratification of human genes into distinct groups enriched for different functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improved mutation rate estimates allow for the stratification of human genes into distinct groups enriched for different functions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is preserved between preprint and publication, though the publication labels it as a result rather than a conclusion; the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning-based localization technique reduces both missing microbubble localization rate and microbubble localization error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning-based localization technique reduces both missing microbubble localization rate and microbubble localization error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core experimental result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Radiofrequency data showed better performance than in-phase quadrature data in the localization study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Radio frequency data showed better performance than in-phase and quadrature data in the localization experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the finding that RF data outperformed IQ data; wording differences are minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based localization technique reduced the vessel microbubble saturation time by more than 50% compared to conventional localization in vivo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning-based localization technique reduced the vessel microbubble saturation time by more than 50% compared to conventional localization in vivo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific quantitative improvement (>50%) is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings support the use of deep learning for more robust and faster microbubble localization, especially under high microbubble concentrations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings support the use of deep learning for more robust and faster microbubble localization, especially under high microbubble concentrations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this concluding statement without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further improvement in localization could be achieved by incorporating temporal information of the microbubble data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further improvement in localization could be achieved by incorporating temporal information of the microbubble data. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The suggestion to incorporate temporal information is present in both versions; the publication frames it as a limitation but the substantive content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural networks exhibited a key signature of surround effects in the early visual cortex, highlighting center stimuli that visually stand out from the surround. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks exhibited a key signature of surround effects in V1, highlighting center stimuli that visually stand out from the surround. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies V1 rather than the broader 'early visual cortex,' which clarifies and slightly narrows the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In some neurons, especially in late layers, when the center stimulus was altered, the most suppressive surround could surprisingly follow. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most suppressive surround can follow changes in the center stimulus in some neurons, especially in late layers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observation with nearly identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The visualization approach generalizes known surround effects for more complex stimuli that have not been revealed in the visual cortex. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The visualization approach generalized previous understanding of surround effects to more complex stimuli, revealing insights not previously observed in visual cortices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes that the visualization revealed new insights and frames it as a broader contribution, clarifying the original conclusion without changing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings provide a testable hypothesis of surround effects for more complex stimuli in higher cortical areas. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results provide a testable hypothesis of surround effects in higher visual cortices. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same hypothesis but with slightly lower stated certainty (medium vs high), so the claim is substantively unchanged but the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The visualization approach could be adopted in biological experimental designs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The visualization approach generalized previous understanding of surround effects to more complex stimuli, revealing insights not previously observed in visual cortices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication foregrounds the generalization and novel insights (stronger contribution framing), which clarifies and slightly amplifies the potential utility for biological experiments compared to the preprint's more tentative adoption suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PARA significantly outperforms existing models on several tasks related to antibody sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PARA significantly outperforms existing models on several tasks related to antibody sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the performance claim as stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PARA has an advantage in capturing antibody sequence information compared to other pre-training models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PARA has an advantage in capturing antibody sequence information compared to other pre-training models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about representation advantage is retained with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The antibody latent representation provided by PARA can substantially facilitate studies in relevant areas. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The antibody latent representation provided by PARA can substantially facilitate studies in relevant areas. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim about the utility of the latent representation; wording and claim content are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: microbeSEG is a user-friendly Python-based cell segmentation tool designed for accurate and automated cell segmentation at the single-cell level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: microbeSEG is a user-friendly Python-based cell segmentation tool designed for accurate and automated cell segmentation at the single-cell level.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim the same contribution claim as the preprint with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: microbeSEG utilizes a state-of-the-art deep learning-based segmentation method that can handle a wide range of cell morphologies and imaging techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: microbeSEG utilizes a state-of-the-art deep learning-based segmentation method for instance segmentation of various cell morphologies and imaging techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the term 'instance segmentation' but retains the same core claim about DL-based segmentation across morphologies and modalities, clarifying the method type.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate cell segmentation results can be obtained within 45 minutes of user time using microbeSEG.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate cell segmentation results can be obtained within 45 minutes of user time using microbeSEG.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried over unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Utilizing public segmentation datasets or pre-labeling can further accelerate the microbeSEG workflow.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing public segmentation datasets or pre-labeling can further accelerate the microbeSEG workflow.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this workflow-acceleration claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: microbeSEG opens the door for accurate and efficient data analysis of microbial cultures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: microbeSEG enables accurate and efficient data analysis of microbial cultures.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conclusion more assertively ('enables' and higher reported certainty), so the claim is clarified and expressed with greater confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The increasing interest in modeling the dynamics of larger proteins reveals a fundamental problem with existing models that describe the molecular system as being in a global configuration state.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims focus on the method and objectives and do not restate this general critique about global-configuration models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of metastable states in large molecular systems grows exponentially with size, limiting the ability to gather sufficient statistics of state probabilities or state-to-state transitions.
alignments.rationale: This specific statement about exponential growth of metastable states and statistical limits does not appear among the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced method combines independent Markov decomposition (IMD) with VAMPnets to address the challenge of modeling large molecular systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a method combining independent Markov decomposition with VAMPnets addresses the challenge of modeling large proteins.
alignments.rationale: Both texts describe the same introduced method (IMD + VAMPnets) addressing the modeling challenge; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training objective established quantifies how well a decomposition of the molecular system into independent subdomains approximates the overall dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The established training objective quantifies the effectiveness of decomposing the molecular system into independent subdomains with Markovian dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the training objective claim more explicit about Markovian dynamics and expresses it with higher reported certainty, clarifying and strengthening the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results represent a significant step towards learning 'Ising models' of large molecular complexes from simulation data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current results represent a significant step towards learning Ising models of large molecular complexes from simulation data.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same concluding claim about progress toward learning Ising models; wording and confidence are consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep-learning model Prot2Prot can quickly imitate photorealistic visualization styles from simpler molecular representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep-learning model Prot2Prot can quickly imitate photorealistic visualization styles from simpler molecular representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Images generated by Prot2Prot are often indistinguishable from those rendered using industry-standard 3D graphics programs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Images generated by Prot2Prot are often indistinguishable from those rendered using industry-standard 3D graphics programs.
alignments.rationale: The claim about visual indistinguishability is carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prot2Prot can create images in a fraction of the time required by traditional rendering methods, even when running in a web browser.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prot2Prot can create images in a fraction of the time required by traditional rendering methods, even when running in a web browser.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same speed claim but lists it with lower reported certainty, so the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prot2Prot is the first example of image-to-image translation applied to macromolecular visualization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prot2Prot is the first example of image-to-image translation applied to macromolecular visualization.
alignments.rationale: Both state Prot2Prot is the first such example; the publication reports this with higher certainty than the preprint, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies significant differences in gut microbiota between colorectal cancer patients with lymph node metastasis and those without.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified significant differences in gut microbiota between colorectal cancer patients with lymph node metastasis and those without.
alignments.rationale: The main result about microbiota differences is retained verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bacteroides plebeius is significantly enriched in the lymph node metastasis group and is associated with the upregulation of resting NK cells and chemokine CXCL8 expression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bacteroides plebeius is significantly enriched in the lymph node metastasis group and is associated with the upregulation of neutrophils and chemokine CXCL8 expression.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the immune cell type associated (preprint: resting NK cells; publication: neutrophils), which is a substantive revision of the result while keeping other elements the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models based on gut microbiota characteristics can accurately predict lymph node metastasis status in colorectal cancer patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models based on gut microbiota characteristics can accurately predict lymph node metastasis status in colorectal cancer patients.
alignments.rationale: The predictive ML model claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that Bacteroides plebeius may play an important role in the progression of colorectal cancer through immune modulation and metabolic pathways.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that Bacteroides plebeius may play an important role in the progression of colorectal cancer through immune modulation and metabolic pathways.
alignments.rationale: The interpretive conclusion about B. plebeius's potential role is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides a new perspective for personalized and precision treatment of colorectal cancer patients based on gut microbiota characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides a new perspective for personalized and precision treatment of colorectal cancer patients based on gut microbiota characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about personalized/precision treatment perspectives remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Out of the 58 test drugs, 14 were predicted by at least five ML models to be useful in managing onchocerciasis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Out of the 58 test drugs, 14 were predicted by at least 5 machine learning models to be potentially useful in managing onchocerciasis.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific ML prediction result with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Molecular docking studies showed binding affinities of the 14 drugs comparable to that of doramectin and pyrvinium.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The binding interactions of cridanimod, diminazene, and vandetanib showed overlaps with doramectin and pyrvinium, which have demonstrated activity against onchocerciasis.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed comparable affinities for the 14 drugs, while the publication focuses on overlaps for three specific agents; this narrows and revises the original docking claim and is reported with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diminazine, trimetinib, triclabendazole, cridanimod, vandetinib and trametinib were the top agents showing high binding affinities from the molecular docking studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cridanimod, diminazene, and vandetanib were identified as the top 3 agents with the highest binding affinities for the glutamate-gated chloride channel, a known target of ivermectin.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the preprint's longer list to a top-3 set (with slightly different spellings/selection) and focuses on a specific target, revising and narrowing the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The binding affinities of diminazine and cridanimod are similar to doramectin and pyrvinium which have demonstrated activity against onchocerciasis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The binding interactions of cridanimod, diminazene, and vandetanib showed overlaps with doramectin and pyrvinium, which have demonstrated activity against onchocerciasis.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes overlapping binding interactions for a slightly different set of compounds (adds vandetanib), changing the specifics of which drugs show similarity and reporting with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The outcome of this study shows the potential of using these 14 drugs to manage onchocerciasis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of cridanimod, diminazene, and vandetanib as promising candidates for developing new treatments for onchocerciasis.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the claimed potential from 14 candidate drugs to three prioritized candidates and expresses the conclusion with higher reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GNNome is a framework for path identification based on geometric deep learning that enables training models on assembly graphs without relying on existing assembly strategies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GNNome is a framework for path identification based on geometric deep learning that enables training models on assembly graphs without relying on existing assembly strategies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are essentially identical in scope and wording, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GNNome reconstructs assemblies with similar or superior contiguity compared to the state-of-the-art tools across several species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GNNome reconstructs assemblies from PacBio HiFi reads with contiguity and quality comparable to those of the state-of-the-art tools across several species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies PacBio HiFi reads and adds quality metrics while replacing 'similar or superior' with 'comparable,' narrowing the data scope and tempering the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework is a plausible cornerstone for future work on reconstructing complex genomes with different ploidy and aneuploidy degrees. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework is a plausible cornerstone for future work on reconstructing complex genomes with different degrees of ploidy and aneuploidy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively the same and the intended conclusion remains unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework and the best-performing model are made publicly available as a tool that can directly be used to assemble new haploid genomes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework and the best-performing model are made publicly available as a tool that can directly be used to assemble new haploid genomes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same availability and intended use claim as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non DL-based baseline methods achieve comparable or superior performance compared to more complex models on survival prediction tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Baseline methods achieve comparable or superior performance compared to more complex models on survival prediction tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about baseline methods on survival prediction with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL representation methods are the most efficient to predict the gene essentiality of cell lines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning representation methods are the most efficient to predict the gene essentiality of cell lines. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing taxonomic NER tools are not suited to the extraction of ecological information from text as they performed poorly on ecologically-oriented corpora. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing taxonomic NER tools are not suited to the extraction of ecological information from text. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TaxoNERD outperformed other approaches on an ecological information extraction task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TaxoNERD outperformed other approaches on an ecological information extraction task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result that TaxoNERD outperformed other approaches is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Efforts are needed to raise ecological information extraction to the same level of performance as its biomedical counterpart. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Efforts are needed to raise ecological information extraction to the same level of performance as its biomedical counterpart. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about the need to improve ecological IE relative to biomedical IE.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Leveraging the huge corpus of unlabelled ecological texts could benefit downstream tasks. (certainty=low, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AnimalMotionViz is an open-source computer vision software tool that allows users to track and visualize dairy cattle movement patterns using a motion heatmap. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AnimalMotionViz is an open-source computer vision software tool that allows users to track and visualize group-level dairy cattle movement patterns using motion maps. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software generates motion heatmaps that can be used to understand space utilization or space occupation by animals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The software generates space-use distribution maps and core and full-range maps that can be used to understand space utilization or space occupation by dairy cattle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the types of maps produced and restricts the target to dairy cattle, clarifying the result and narrowing its scope from 'animals' generally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In four 5 min sample videos, quadrants with peak intensity of space use, as identified using the software, aligned with quadrants where calves spent the greatest duration of time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In four 5-min sample videos, quadrants with peak intensity of space use, as identified using the software, aligned with quadrants where calves spent the greatest duration of time. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AnimalMotionViz can assess how space allocation affects dairy cow movement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AnimalMotionViz has the potential for broader application in studying the movement patterns of other animal species beyond dairy cattle. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cytomulate is a reproducible and accurate simulation algorithm of CyTOF data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cytomulate is a reproducible and accurate simulation algorithm for CyTOF data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cytomulate can capture various characteristics of CyTOF data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cytomulate can capture various characteristics of CyTOF data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cytomulate is superior in learning overall data distributions compared to single-cell RNA-seq-oriented methods such as scDesign2, Splatter, and generative models like LAMBDA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cytomulate is superior in learning overall data distributions compared to single-cell RNA-seq-oriented methods and generative models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The input and output layers of the mouse cerebellar cortex exhibit redundant and selective connectivity motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The input and output layers of the mouse cerebellar cortex exhibit redundant and selective connectivity motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim the same result as the preprint, with no substantive change in meaning or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical simulations suggest that redundant, non-random connectivity motifs increase discriminability of similar input patterns at a minimal cost to the network’s overall encoding capacity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Redundant, non-random connectivity motifs increase the resilience to noise at a negligible cost to the overall encoding capacity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work reveals how neuronal network structure can balance encoding capacity and redundancy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific wording about balancing encoding capacity and redundancy does not appear in the publication claims, indicating the explicit formulation was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings have implications for artificial neural network design. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work reveals principles of biological network architecture that have implications for the design of artificial neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same contribution in slightly broader terms (principles of architecture) but retains the implication for ANN design, so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transcranial temporal interfering stimulation (tTIS) can focally stimulate deep parts of the brain related to specific functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transcranial temporal interfering stimulation (tTIS) can focally stimulate deep parts of the brain related to specific functions using beats at two high frequencies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method using an unsupervised neural network (USNN) for tTIS can optimize the interfering current value of high-definition electrodes quickly. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method using an unsupervised neural network (USNN) can optimize the interfering current value of high-definition electrodes quickly and accurately. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit claim about accuracy in addition to speed, strengthening the confidence dimension while keeping the optimization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method generated the strongest stimulation on the target, even when targeting deep areas or multi-target stimulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method generated the strongest stimulation at the target, even when targeting deep areas or performing multi-target stimulation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optimization of a target could be performed in 3 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimization of the electrode currents for the target stimulus could be performed in 3 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same timing result with the same meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The possibility of stimulating the deep part of the brain precisely with transcranial electrical stimulation was confirmed. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-definition tTIS was less affected than transcranial alternating current stimulation (tACS) by target depth, and mis-stimulation was reduced. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated high-throughput scoring system demonstrates a high and significant correlation with manually scored disease severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The shallow convolutional neural-network (SCNN) trained in this study demonstrates a high and significant correlation with manually scored disease severity. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The shallow convolutional neural-network (SCNN) was trained for efficient detection of leaf disc segments showing P. viticola sporangiophores. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We report a system for automated high-throughput scoring of disease severity on inoculated leaf discs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The leaf disc-scoring pipeline has the potential to considerably reduce the time required for leaf disc phenotyping. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The leaf disc-scoring pipeline has the potential to considerably reduce the amount of time during leaf disc phenotyping. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pipeline is freely available for adaptation to other pathogens. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline is freely available for adaptation to other pathogens. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the availability claim verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The one-step object detection by YOLOv4 alone resulted in a precision of 0.55 when classifying two species of flea beetles and background objects. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed two-stage CNN method improved the precision to 0.89 with moderate accuracy (F-measure = 0.55) and acceptable speed (approximately 5 frames per second for full HD images). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The customized CNN-based method improved the precision of insect identification from 0.55 to 0.89. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method aids in improving small object detection on a heterogeneous background. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves an acceptable speed of detection and identification at approximately 5 frames per second for full HD images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unprioritized memory items (UMI) are actively represented in the brain in a 'flipped' format compared to prioritized memory items (PMI). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unprioritized memory items (UMI) are actively represented in the brain in a 'flipped' format compared to prioritized memory items (PMI). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of demixed PCA to the data revealed a cascade of multiple trajectories in UMI and PMI subspaces, each with a different time course. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Information held in working memory undergoes priority-based transformations that allow for its retention while preventing interference with concurrent behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transformation allows for retention of unprioritized information in working memory while preventing it from interfering with concurrent behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work illustrates the value of artificial neural networks for assessing and refining hypotheses about mechanisms for information processing in the brain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature-disentangled w representations outperform both z and CLIP representations in explaining neural responses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature-disentangled w representations outperform both z and CLIP representations in explaining neural responses. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: W-latent features are positioned at the higher end of the complexity gradient, indicating they capture visual information relevant to high-level neural activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A multivariate neural decoding analysis of the feature-disentangled representations resulted in state-of-the-art spatiotemporal reconstructions of visual perception. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the important role of feature-disentanglement in shaping high-level neural representations underlying visual perception. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results highlight the important role of feature-disentanglement in shaping high-level neural representations underlying visual perception. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed workflow fully automates the process of in situ cryo electron tomography. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed smart parallel automated cryo-electron tomography (SPACEtomo) workflow fully automates the cryo-electron tomography process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The automation provided by SPACEtomo is essential for obtaining statistically relevant datasets and high-resolution structures of macromolecules. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hierarchical reinforcement learning agent can learn and transfer individual task components as well as entire structures by inferring through a non-parametric Bayesian model of the task. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hierarchical reinforcement learning agent can learn and transfer individual task components as well as entire structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed agent maintains a factorised representation of task components through a hierarchical Dirichlet process. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach improves generalisation and transfer in more complex, hierarchical tasks with goal/subgoal structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach improves generalisation and transfer in complex, hierarchical tasks with goal/subgoal structures. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clustering algorithm proposed could conceivably be implemented by cortico-striatal gating circuits in the brain. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clustering algorithm proposed could be implemented by cortico-striatal gating circuits in the brain. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GePI is a database portal for fully automated extraction and presentation of molecular interaction facts from scientific literature. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: NLIMED's approach for chunking and annotating queries is more effective than the NCBO Annotator for identifying relevant ontology classes in natural language queries. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The COmputational Modeling in BIology NEtwork (COMBINE) community recommends the use of the Resource Description Framework (RDF) for semantic annotation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The COmputational Modeling in BIology NEtwork (COMBINE) community recommends the use of the Resource Description Framework (RDF) for semantic annotation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MAP algorithm achieved higher or similar AUC and F-scores compared to the ICD code across all 16 phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MAP algorithm achieved higher or similar AUC and F-scores compared to the ICD code across all 16 phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The features assembled via the automated approach had comparable accuracy to those assembled via manual curation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The features assembled via the automated approach had comparable accuracy to those assembled via manual curation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The PheWAS results suggest that the MAP approach detected previously validated associations with higher power when compared to the standard PheWAS method based on ICD codes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The MAP approach increased the accuracy of phenotype definition while maintaining scalability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The drift-diffusion model (DDM) can be extended to accommodate multiple alternatives in decision making. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The drift–diffusion model (DDM) can be extended to accommodate multiple alternatives in decision making. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The competition between n alternatives occurs in a linear subspace of n-1 dimensions, involving n-1 decision variables coupled through correlated noise sources. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The competition between n alternatives occurs in a linear subspace of n−1 dimensions with n−1 coupled decision variables. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model describing a lower dimensional diffusion process can be derived from a nonlinear neuronal system. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model describing a lower-dimensional diffusion process can be derived from a nonlinear neuronal system. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamics of the nonlinear DDM can be represented as the motion of a particle on a potential, with an analytically given general form for any number of alternatives. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dynamics of the nonlinear DDM can be represented as the motion of a particle on a potential, which is analytically defined for any number of alternatives. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-attribute, time-dependent drift diffusion model (mtDDM) demonstrates that taste and health differentially influence the evidence accumulation process in dietary choices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-attribute, time-dependent drift diffusion model demonstrates that taste and health differentially influence the evidence accumulation process in dietary choices. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different decision attributes shape both the relative value signal and the latency of evidence accumulation in a manner consistent with participants’ idiosyncratic preferences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attributes of taste and health shape both the relative value signal and the latency of evidence accumulation in a manner consistent with participants’ idiosyncratic preferences. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A healthy choice intervention alters mtDDM parameters that predict prime-dependent choices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A healthy choice intervention alters multi-attribute, time-dependent drift diffusion model parameters, which predict prime-dependent choices. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide new insights into the construction of interventions to alter the processes of choice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different decision attributes make separable contributions to the strength and timing of evidence accumulation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our biologically-plausible learning rules were effective in training spiking neuronal network models to solve problems in dynamic environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our biologically-plausible learning rules were effective in training spiking neuronal network models to solve problems in dynamic environments. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different circuit architectures and learning rules produced similar performance in sensory-motor tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning mechanisms involving different neural circuits produce similar performance in sensory-motor tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new biologically-inspired learning rule significantly enhanced performance while reducing training time. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning mechanisms involving different neural circuits may complement one another, accelerating the learning capabilities of animals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of topological data analysis and geometric deep learning provides a comprehensive understanding of protein structural motifs within protein pockets. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation learning framework for macromolecules is particularly useful when the structure is known and relies on both local and global information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation learning framework is particularly useful when the structure of macromolecules is known and relies on both local and global information. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study profiles the predictive power of coupling local and global representations for discriminating between enzymes and non-enzymes. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellGO can effectively capture cell type-specific pathways even when working with mixed cell-type markers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: SpecGAN enables two orders of magnitude higher temporal resolution compared to conventional single-molecule spectrometers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The super-resolution spectrum image of the COS-7 membrane can be reconstructed with merely 12,000 frames of single-molecule localization images using SpecGAN. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed an attention-based deep learning model to design peptides or protein fragment sequences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed interfaces accurately capture essential native interactions and have comparable native-like binding affinities in silico. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model does not need a precise backbone location, making it an attractive tool for working with de novo design of protein-protein interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computed physically informed features bear considerably larger correlation with cleavage activity compared to experimental epigenetic features. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: RNA-GAN demonstrates the ability to generalize to gene expression profiles outside of the training set, indicating imputation capabilities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A statistical test can be used to deduce minimal models for the syllable sequences in Bengalese finch songs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scVI and scANVI compare favorably to the existing methods for data integration and cell state annotation in terms of accuracy, scalability, and adaptability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scVI and scANVI compare favorably to state-of-the-art methods for data integration and cell state annotation in terms of accuracy, scalability, and adaptability. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analytical pipeline for finding and following individual platelets over time in growing thrombi is more accurate than previously described methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepDynaForecast demonstrates the utility of phylodynamic trees for infection forecasting and backtracking. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepDynaForecast was trained on in-depth simulation data and utilizes phylogenetic tree information more effectively than any other algorithm in the field. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Every tested architecture succeeded in reducing the transcriptomes to 50 latent dimensions, which captured enough variation for accurate reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning techniques, specifically autoencoder architectures, can learn latent variables that drive transcriptome signals. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint gives a specific finding about reduction to 50 dimensions; the publication frames a broader conclusion about autoencoders learning latent variables, clarifying and generalizing the specific benchmark result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simple, fully connected autoencoder performs best across the benchmarks, but lacks the characteristic of having directly interpretable latent dimensions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simple, fully connected autoencoder performs best across the benchmarks but lacks directly interpretable latent dimensions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same comparative performance claim and the same note about interpretability with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study opens a new direction for differential pathway analysis in transcriptomics with increased transparency and interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study opens a new direction for differential pathway analysis in transcriptomics with increased transparency and interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same contribution claim about opening a new direction in pathway analysis with similar emphasis and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We can accurately predict the distance matrix of a protein by deep learning, even for proteins with ∼60 sequence homologs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We can accurately predict interresidue distance distribution of a protein by deep learning, even for proteins with ∼60 sequence homologs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with nearly identical meaning (distance matrix vs interresidue distance distribution) and same population of homolog counts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method successfully folded 21 of the 37 CASP12 hard targets with a median family size of 58 effective sequence homologs within 4 hours on a Linux computer of 20 CPUs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method successfully folded 21 of the 37 CASP12 hard targets with a median family size of 58 effective sequence homologs within 4 h on a Linux computer of 20 central processing units. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact performance claim with only minor wording changes (hours/CPU wording), preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rigorous experimental validation in CASP13 shows that our distance-based folding server successfully folded 17 of 32 hard targets and obtained 70% precision on top L/5 long-range predicted contacts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rigorous experimental validation in CASP13 shows that our distance-based folding server successfully folded 17 of 32 hard targets and obtained 70% precision on the top L/5 long-range predicted contacts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the CASP13 results verbatim; no substantive change in content or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our server predicted correct fold for two membrane proteins of new fold while all the other servers failed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The latest experimental validation in CAMEO shows that our server predicted correct folds for 2 membrane proteins while all of the other servers failed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core result (two membrane proteins predicted when others failed) but drops the explicit 'new fold' phrasing and frames it as a CAMEO validation, narrowing the novelty claim while keeping the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is now feasible to predict correct fold for proteins lacking similar structures in PDB on a personal computer without folding simulation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results demonstrate that it is now feasible to predict correct fold for many more proteins lack of similar structures in the Protein Data Bank even on a personal computer. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the feasibility conclusion with stronger certainty and broader wording ('many more proteins') while omitting the specific 'without folding simulation' constraint from the preprint, thus broadening scope and amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ParaSurf significantly enhances paratope prediction by incorporating both surface geometric and non-geometric factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ParaSurf significantly enhances paratope prediction by incorporating both surface geometric and non-geometric factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication about method improvements and factors incorporated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ParaSurf achieves state-of-the-art results across nearly all metrics when trained and tested on three prominent antibody-antigen benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ParaSurf achieves state-of-the-art results across nearly all metrics when trained and tested on three prominent antibody-antigen benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ParaSurf demonstrates the ability to accurately predict binding scores across the entire Fab region of the antibody. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unlike models restricted to the variable region, ParaSurf demonstrates the ability to accurately predict binding scores across the entire Fab region of the antibody. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit contrast to models limited to the variable region, clarifying the novelty of predicting across the full Fab, but preserves the substantive claim and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An extensive analysis was conducted focusing on paratope prediction for each Complementarity-Determining Region loop, the performance of models trained exclusively on the heavy chain, and the results of training models solely on the light chain. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This methodological/analysis-detail claim present in the preprint is not represented in the publication's listed claims, indicating it was omitted from the final claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepCellMap captures the morphological diversity of microglia and identifies strong coupling between proliferative and phagocytic phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepCellMap captures the morphological diversity of microglia during brain development. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the morphological-diversity claim (with an explicit brain-development context); the meaning is consistent with the preprint's morphology statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepCellMap captures the morphological diversity of microglia and identifies strong coupling between proliferative and phagocytic phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a strong coupling between proliferative and phagocytic phenotypes of microglia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication extracts and states explicitly the coupling claim that was combined with morphology in the preprint; content and certainty are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct spatial clusters of microglia rarely overlap as human brain development progresses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct spatial clusters of microglia rarely overlap as human brain development progresses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The spatial-clustering claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a novel association between microglia and blood vessels in fetal brains exposed to maternal SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An association exists between microglia and blood vessels in fetal brains exposed to maternal SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the association claim with similar wording and the same confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepCellMap is a powerful tool for extracting spatial statistics and analyzing cellular organization in large tissue sections. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepCellMap is a powerful tool for extracting spatial statistics and analyzing cellular organization in large tissue sections. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The tool/contribution claim is directly preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based image classification of dying cells can analyze programmed cell death in single Herpes Simplex Virus-1-infected cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based image classification of dying cells can analyze programmed cell death in single herpes simplex virus-1 (HSV-1)-infected cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim with only minor capitalization/parenthetical abbreviation differences; meaning and confidence unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Despite heterogeneous activation of signaling, individual cells adopt predominantly prototypical death morphologies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Despite heterogeneous activation of signaling, individual cells adopt predominantly prototypical death morphologies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Programmed cell death is executed heterogeneously within a uniform population of virus-infected cells and varies over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Programmed cell death is executed heterogeneously within a uniform population of virus-infected cells and varies over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this temporal heterogeneity result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Image-based phenotyping can provide valuable insights into cell fate decisions, complementing molecular assays. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Image-based phenotyping can provide valuable insights into cell fate decisions, complementing molecular assays. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MSRCall outperforms other basecallers in terms of both read and consensus accuracies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MSRCall outperforms other basecallers in terms of both read and consensus accuracies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance claim is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The calling accuracy of MinION still needs to be improved compared to NGS basecallers. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The calling accuracy of MinION still needs to be improved compared to NGS basecallers. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Limitation statement is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MSRCall comprises a multi-scale structure, recurrent layers, a fusion block, and a CTC decoder. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recurrent layer of MSRCall is redesigned to capture various time-scale features with a multi-scale structure. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses and clarifies the architectural novelty on the recurrent layer and multi-scale structure, omitting some components (fusion block, CTC decoder) mentioned in the preprint and narrowing the scope of the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNAPE achieved over 90% accuracy in the prediction of arm-level CNAs using brain tumors as a proof-of-concept study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNAPE achieved over 80 percent accuracy in predicting DNA copy number of chromosomes, chromosomal arms, and 12 commonly altered genes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a lower quantified accuracy (>80% vs >90%) and frames results across chromosomes, arms, and genes (broader scope) rather than specifically arm-level in brain tumors, so the claim is revised and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNAPE demonstrated reasonable accuracy in predicting 12 gene-level CNAs commonly altered in glioma.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNAPE achieved over 80 percent accuracy in predicting DNA copy number of chromosomes, chromosomal arms, and 12 commonly altered genes.
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies the previously vague 'reasonable accuracy' (>80%) and reports prediction for the same 12 genes but in a broader dataset (not limited to glioma), increasing specificity and apparent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNAPE is an alternative method for inferring CNA using gene expression data, addressing limitations of current high-throughput CNA detection methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNAPE can computationally infer copy number alterations from gene expression data.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms CNAPE infers CNAs from expression data (same functional claim) but does not explicitly state it addresses limitations of current high-throughput CNA detection methods, so the contribution is presented in a narrower form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The connection of Electronic Health Records (EHRs) to the Scalable Precision Medicine Oriented Knowledge Engine (SPOKE) represents a significant advancement in leveraging multi-dimensional data for patient care.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Electronic Health Records (EHRs) of individual patients can be connected to a heterogeneous knowledge network called Scalable Precision Medicine Oriented Knowledge Engine (SPOKE).
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution—connecting EHRs to SPOKE—without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An unsupervised machine-learning algorithm was successfully used to create Propagated SPOKE Entry Vectors (PSEVs) that encode the importance of each SPOKE node for any code in the EHRs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An unsupervised machine-learning algorithm creates Propagated SPOKE Entry Vectors (PSEVs) that encode the importance of each SPOKE node for any code in the EHRs.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same methodological/result claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of PSEVs into any EHR machine-learning platform is a key step toward advancing precision medicine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of PSEVs into any EHR machine-learning platform provides a key step toward precision medicine.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the conclusion but presents it with higher asserted certainty, so confidence is amplified while the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MIEL has superior resolution compared to conventional intensity thresholding techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIEL captures the nuclear staining patterns of epigenetic marks and employs machine learning to accurately distinguish between such patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication explains how MIEL distinguishes staining patterns and reports accurate discrimination, but it does not explicitly claim a direct superiority comparison to thresholding, so the original comparative claim is clarified and effectively narrowed/tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MIEL enables efficient detection of epigenetically active compounds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIEL can identify and classify epigenetically active drugs.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same capability to detect/identify epigenetically active compounds (drugs), with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MIEL can flag possible off-target effects and predict novel drug function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIEL was able to accurately rank candidate drugs by their ability to produce desired epigenetic alterations.
alignments.rationale: The publication documents accurate ranking of candidate drugs for epigenetic effect but does not claim off-target detection or prediction of novel functions, so the broader predictive/off-target claim is revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MIEL platform was validated across multiple cell lines and using dose-response curves.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MIEL platform has been validated across multiple cell lines and using dose-response curves.
alignments.rationale: Validation across cell lines and dose-response curves is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new methodology combines Structured Illumination Microscopy (SIM) with machine learning algorithms to image and classify the structure of large populations of biopharmaceutical viruses with high resolution.
