alignments.pub_claim_text: A new methodology combining Structured Illumination Microscopy with machine learning algorithms enables high-resolution imaging and classification of biopharmaceutical viruses.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the combined SIM+ML methodology and its high-resolution imaging/classification capability with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method offers information on virus morphology that can ultimately be linked with functional performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method provides information on virus morphology that can be linked to functional performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same link between morphology information and potential functional performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method enables the rapid assessment of the quality of viral production with high throughput, obviating the need for traditional batch testing methods which are complex and time consuming.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This unique tool allows for the rapid assessment of viral production quality, eliminating the need for complex and time-consuming traditional batch testing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim about rapid, high-throughput assessment replacing traditional batch testing with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method also works on non-purified samples from pooled harvest fluids directly from the production line.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is effective on non-purified samples from pooled harvest fluids directly from the production line.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same result regarding effectiveness on non-purified pooled harvest fluids.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 43% of Human Accelerated Regions (HARs) have variants with large opposing effects on chromatin state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 43% of human accelerated regions (HARs) have variants with large opposing effects on chromatin state.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative result about HARs and chromatin state.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 14% of HARs have variants with large opposing effects on neurodevelopmental enhancer activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 14% of HARs have variants with large opposing effects on neurodevelopmental enhancer activity.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative finding about enhancer activity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The species-specific enhancer activity of assayed HARs was accurately predicted from the presence and absence of transcription factor footprints in each species.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The species-specific enhancer activity of HARs was accurately predicted from the presence and absence of transcription factor footprints in each species.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the predictive result and its basis (TF footprints) without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HARs did not evolve to compensate for changes in the trans environment but instead altered their ability to bind factors present in both species.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HARs did not evolve to compensate for changes in the trans environment but instead altered their ability to bind factors present in both species.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the evolutionary interpretation identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phytoplankton cell density in a lake is highly predictable over short time scales, with model R² values decreasing from 0.89 at 4 hours to 0.75 at 1 month.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phytoplankton cell density in a lake is highly predictable over hours to months, with model R² values decreasing from 0.89 at 4 hours to 0.74 at 1 month.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with nearly identical numeric values (0.75→0.74) and slightly broader wording (hours to months), so the claim is effectively the same but clarified with a small numeric adjustment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In a long-term dataset, the predictability of phytoplankton cell density decreases from 0.46 at 1 month to 0.32 at 10 years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In a long-term dataset lacking fine spatial resolution, the predictability of phytoplankton cell density decreases from 0.46 at 1 month to 0.32 at 10 years.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric result but adds a dataset limitation (lacking fine spatial resolution), which narrows the claim's scope and clarifies its applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model-inferred environmental growth dependencies for cyanobacterial and eukaryotic algal cell density matched laboratory studies and suggested novel trade-offs governing their competition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-inferred environmental growth dependencies for cyanobacterial and eukaryotic algal cell densities matched laboratory studies and suggested novel trade-offs governing their competition.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this conclusion verbatim (minor pluralization) with the same level of certainty and interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-frequency monitoring and machine learning can elucidate the mechanisms underlying ecological dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-frequency monitoring and machine learning can set prediction targets for process-based models and help elucidate the mechanisms underlying ecological dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the contribution by adding a practical application (setting prediction targets for process-based models) while retaining the original claim about elucidating mechanisms, thus clarifying and broadening scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We adapt optimization algorithms from machine learning to rapidly screen and design gene circuits capable of performing arbitrary functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We adapted machine-learning algorithms to significantly accelerate gene circuit discovery.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same core contribution (using ML algorithms to speed gene-circuit work) with slightly different phrasing (accelerate discovery vs. rapidly screen/design), so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the power of this approach by designing circuits that recapitulate important in vivo phenomena, such as oscillators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found that we could rapidly design circuits capable of executing a range of different functions, including those that recapitulate important in vivo phenomena, such as oscillators.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result, explicitly noting oscillators as an example; content and confidence are effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method can be readily applied to biological networks of any type and size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method can be readily applied to biological networks of any type and size.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this generalizability claim verbatim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We also design circuits to perform complex tasks for synthetic biology, such as counting noisy biological events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found that we could rapidly design circuits capable of executing a range of different functions, including those that recapitulate important in vivo phenomena, such as oscillators.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes a range of functions but does not mention the specific 'counting noisy events' result; the specific task appears de-emphasized or generalized, so the claim is revised and effectively narrowed in specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is provided as an open-source and easy-to-use python module, GeneNet.
alignments.rationale: The publication's top-level claims do not mention the open-source GeneNet module; this specific contribution appears omitted from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Python codebase for random-forest machine learning segmentation significantly reduces the time required to process single leaf microCT scans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Python codebase for random forest machine learning segmentation reduces the time required to process single-leaf microCT scans.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (time reduction for processing microCT scans) with essentially identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves over 90% accuracy in background and tissue segmentation by training on 6 hand segmented image slices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves over 90% accuracy in background and tissue segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the >90% accuracy result but omits the specific training-detail about 6 hand segmented slices, reducing methodological specificity and thus clarifying/streamlining the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This 3D segmentation and quantification pipeline addresses a major barrier to using microCT imaging in high-throughput plant phenotyping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This 3D segmentation and quantification pipeline can reduce barriers to using microCT imaging in high-throughput plant phenotyping.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the strength (from 'addresses a major barrier' to 'can reduce barriers'), so the semantic content is clarified while the expressed confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We introduce a generalization of McCaskill’s algorithm that is well-defined over continuous inputs and is differentiable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce a generalization of McCaskill's algorithm that is well-defined over continuous inputs.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the contributions across multiple claims; this claim keeps the continuous-input generalization but separates out differentiability into a different claim, so the original is slightly clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This generalization allows us to tackle the inverse folding problem directly using gradient optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the gradient to optimize the sequence directly is sufficient to arrive at sequences with a high probability of folding into the desired structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same ability to solve inverse folding by gradient optimization, but with a lower stated certainty (medium), so the semantic content is preserved while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work highlights how principles from differentiable programming can be translated to existing physical models to develop powerful tools for machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gradients we compute are meaningful.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a specific aspect (meaningful gradients) that instantiates the broader claim about translating differentiable programming principles, narrowing and clarifying the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a concrete example by implementing an effective and interpretable RNA design algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This generalization enables an end-to-end differentiable partition function calculation.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the concrete implementation in terms of enabling end-to-end differentiable partition function calculations; the more practical 'effective interpretable algorithm' claim is preserved in spirit but expressed with lower certainty, so it is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software framework allows for the alignment-based training, evaluation, and application of machine learning models with user-defined parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The software framework allows for the alignment-based training, evaluation, and application of machine learning models with user-defined parameters.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this software-framework capability verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework provides a structured generation and evaluation of models based on arbitrary features and input data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework provides a structured generation and evaluation of models based on arbitrary features and input data.
alignments.rationale: This contribution is restated identically in the publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software package was used in a full-genome screen of Drosophila melanogaster and the results were evaluated against the program RNAz.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results obtained from the software package were evaluated against the RNAz program, which is less flexible.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the result-evaluation mapping to RNAz but adds context (RNAz is less flexible), which clarifies the comparison without changing the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CheckM2 is an improved method of predicting the completeness and contamination of metagenome-assembled genomes (MAGs) using machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CheckM2 outperforms existing tools in both accuracy and computational speed.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the improvement more specifically (accuracy and computational speed) compared with the preprint's general statement about completeness/contamination prediction, thus clarifying and broadening the improvement to include speed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CheckM2 outperforms the original version of CheckM in predicting MAG quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CheckM2 outperforms existing tools in both accuracy and computational speed.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the outperformance claim more explicit (accuracy and speed) and generalizes to existing tools; this clarifies the original preprint claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CheckM2 accurately predicts genome quality for MAGs from novel lineages, including those with sparse genomic representation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CheckM2 accurately predicts genome quality for MAGs from novel lineages, even for those with reduced genome size.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert accuracy for novel lineages; the publication replaces "sparse genomic representation" with the specific case of "reduced genome size," which narrows/clarifies the claim's focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CheckM2 provides accurate genome quality predictions across the microbial tree of life.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CheckM2 provides accurate genome quality predictions across bacterial and archaeal lineages.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies bacterial and archaeal lineages rather than the broader "microbial tree of life," making the claim more specific (narrowed) but consistent in intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiCognition is a visual exploration and machine-learning tool that enables detection of patterns and associations between 3D chromosome conformation and collections of 1D genomics profiles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiCognition is a visual exploration and machine-learning tool that enables detection of patterns and associations between 3D chromosome conformation and collections of 1D genomics profiles.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The flexible user interface and machine learning tools of HiCognition can help understand the relationship between structure and function of the genome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The flexible user interface and machine learning tools of HiCognition help to understand the relationship between the structure and function of the genome.
alignments.rationale: The claim is substantively the same, but the publication reports a lower certainty (medium vs high), so the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiCognition reveals how transcriptional activity and cohesin subunit isoforms contribute to chromosome conformation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiCognition reveals how transcription and cohesion subunit isoforms contribute to chromosome conformation.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same result claim with higher stated certainty (medium→high), so the content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated assay (AVIA™) can quantify infection phenotypes within hours, before they are manually visible, and without sample preparation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated viral infectivity assay (AVIA™) can quantify infection phenotypes within hours, before they are manually visible, and without sample preparation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this functional performance claim directly and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML models were trained on a variety of viruses, including HIV, influenza A virus, and coronaviruses, covering the four major categories of virus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNN models trained on various viruses demonstrate sensitivity ranges comparable to or better than conventional plaque or TCID50 assays.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes model sensitivity/performance but does not restate the detailed training-set composition; I map the preprint claim to this performance-focused publication claim with moderate confidence due to the omitted virus list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sigmoidal function fit to virus dilution curves yielded an R 2 higher than 0.98 and a linear dynamic range comparable to or better than conventional assays.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The precision of the AVIA™ is approximately 10%, which is considerably better than conventional infectivity assays.
alignments.rationale: The preprint reports R2 and dynamic range metrics, while the publication reports precision (~10%) as the comparative metric; the performance claim is retained but the measurement/statistic has been revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This technology may have potential as a rapid, broad-spectrum tool for virus identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This technology has potential as a rapid, broad-spectrum tool for virus characterization and potentially identification.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the stated potential to explicitly include characterization in addition to identification, clarifying and slightly broadening the intended applications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We project 3-9 fold improvements in prediction performance for behavioral and mental health phenotypes when moving from one thousand to one million samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A 3- to 9-fold improvement in prediction performance occurs when sample size increases from 1,000 to 1 million participants.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the numerical projection directly and with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Moving from single imaging modalities to multimodal input data can lead to further improvements in prediction performance, often on par with doubling the sample size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating multiple imaging modalities boosts prediction accuracy, often equivalent to doubling the sample size.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about multimodal integration providing improvements comparable to doubling sample size, with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analyses reveal considerable performance reserves for neuroimaging-based phenotype prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prediction accuracy remains worryingly low and far from fully exploiting the predictive potential of brain imaging data.
alignments.rationale: Both express that current performance is far from potential; the publication frames this more starkly ("worryingly low"), clarifying the concern without changing scope or confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models may benefit much more from extremely large neuroimaging datasets than currently believed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Achieving substantial improvements in phenotype prediction may necessitate prohibitively large sample sizes, casting doubt on the practical or clinical utility of machine learning in some areas of neuroimaging.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's optimistic projection is contrasted in the publication by a cautionary limitation about practicality and clinical utility, representing a substantive revision and tempering of confidence about real-world benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Fiscore package implements a pipeline for Gaussian mixture modelling, making machine learning methods accessible to non-experts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Fiscore package implements a pipeline for Gaussian mixture modelling that makes machine learning methods accessible to non-experts.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the package's implementation and accessibility goal directly and unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Probabilistic machine learning techniques can help with a better interpretation of complex biological phenomena related to protein function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Probabilistic machine learning techniques can help with a better interpretation of complex biological phenomena related to protein function.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about probabilistic ML aiding interpretation is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fiscore builds on the mathematical formulation of protein physicochemical properties that can aid in drug discovery and target evaluation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fiscore builds on the mathematical formulation of protein physicochemical properties that can aid in drug discovery and target evaluation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this applied-utility claim; wording and intent are the same (minor label change from result to contribution does not alter the claim content).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The package provides interactive environments to explore various features of interest in protein analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The package provides interactive environments to explore various features of interest.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of interactive environments is restated with minimal wording change and the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transcriptional regulatory network (TRN) of Bacillus subtilis coordinates cellular functions including metabolism, biofilm formation, and sporulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transcriptional regulatory network (TRN) of Bacillus subtilis coordinates cellular functions including metabolism, biofilm formation, and sporulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are effectively identical in content and certainty about TRN coordinating those functions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We obtained 83 independently modulated gene sets that explain most of the variance in expression, with 76% representing the effects of known regulators. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We obtained 83 independently modulated gene sets that explain most of the variance in expression and demonstrate that 76% of them represent the effects of known regulators. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report 83 modules explaining most variance and that ~76% correspond to known regulators; wording in the publication slightly clarifies demonstration but does not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TRN structure and its condition-dependent activity uncover novel or recently discovered roles for at least 5 regulons. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TRN structure and its condition-dependent activity uncover putative or recently discovered roles for at least five regulons. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces 'novel' with the more cautious term 'putative,' making the claim more tentative while keeping the core finding (≥5 regulons) the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TRN facilitates quantification of population-level sporulation states, revealing a putative anaerobic metabolism role for SigG. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TRN facilitates quantification of population-level sporulation states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the quantification claim but omits the specific SigG/anaerobic metabolism finding, narrowing the scope and removing that specific result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TRN could inform research on nearly every aspect of transcriptional regulation in Bacillus subtilis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TRN could inform research on nearly every aspect of transcriptional regulation in B. subtilis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim but downgrades certainty (high→medium), making the claim more cautious without changing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Infant chimpanzee distress calls are highly graded and convey information about discrete events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Infant chimpanzee distress calls can convey information about discrete problems experienced by the infant. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core result that calls convey discrete problem information but drops 'highly graded' and reduces certainty, making the claim less strong.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The acoustic variants produced by infant chimpanzees predicted maternal responses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The acoustic variants of distress calls are associated with particular maternal responses. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's stronger wording ('predicted') is softened in the publication to 'are associated with,' and certainty is reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Maternal parenting decisions are guided by the information conveyed in distress calls. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mothers may rely on acoustic cues to guide their parenting decisions. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication couches the causal claim more tentatively ('may rely') and assigns lower certainty, softening the conclusion that parenting decisions are guided by calls.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study addresses the evolution of vocal flexibility in the human lineage. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present a claim about addressing the evolution of vocal flexibility in the human lineage; that contribution is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Infant chimpanzee distress calls provide information about the distance to the mother. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim (distance to the mother) is not present in the preprint and appears as a newly reported result in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Signal features that are highly informative for decoding somatosensory stimuli appear underutilised in neuroprosthetic applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest performing individual feature, spike amplitude, classified somatosensory DCN signals with 70% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete performance metrics for informative features (e.g., spike amplitude) that support the preprint's general point about informative features, but it does not explicitly state the underutilisation in neuroprosthetics, so the claim is clarified with quantitative detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proprioception-dominated stimuli generalise across animals better than tactile-dominated stimuli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proprioception-dominated stimuli generalize across animals better than tactile-dominated stimuli. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core finding is the same, but the publication frames it slightly more cautiously (medium certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Information that signal features contribute to neural decoding changes over a time-course of dynamic somatosensory events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Information that signal features contribute to neural decoding changes over the time-course of dynamic somatosensory events. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same temporal dynamics result but with slightly lower certainty, converting a high-confidence preprint result into a more cautious conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings may improve neural decoding for various applications including novel neuroprosthetic design. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature set investigated may also prove useful for decoding other neural signals, such as motor signals. (certainty=low, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows and softens the potential applications (specifically mentioning motor signals) and expresses the possibility more cautiously, reducing the breadth and confidence of the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existence of paratope-epitope interaction motifs is a fundamental premise for the predictability of antibody-antigen binding. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The existence of paratope-epitope interaction motifs is a fundamental premise for the predictability of antibody-antigen binding. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this foundational conclusion verbatim and at the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified structural interaction motifs that compose a commonly shared structure-based vocabulary of paratope-epitope interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify structural interaction motifs that compose a commonly shared structure-based vocabulary of paratope-epitope interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The identification of structural interaction motifs is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The vocabulary of paratope-epitope interactions enables the machine learnability of antibody-antigen binding on the paratope-epitope level using generative machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine-learning-driven predictive paratope and epitope engineering is feasible. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication summarizes the preprint's demonstration of learnability as feasibility for ML-driven engineering; the core claim about ML applicability is preserved though phrased more concisely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The vocabulary is compact, consisting of less than 10,000 motifs, and is distinct from non-immune protein-protein interactions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The vocabulary of paratope-epitope interactions is compact, consisting of less than 10<sup>4</sup> motifs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the compactness claim (same numeric bound) and presents it with higher certainty; the distinctness aspect is also reported (see next alignment) so the content is preserved and in some cases strengthened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The vocabulary is compact, consisting of less than 10,000 motifs, and is distinct from non-immune protein-protein interactions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The vocabulary is distinct from non-immune protein-protein interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the distinctness from non-immune PPIs and does so with higher certainty than the preprint; the preprint contained both compactness and distinctness, so both publication claims match that content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Levels of pS396 tau and Aβ were significantly elevated in AD EVs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Levels of pS396 tau and Aβ1-42 were significantly elevated in AD EVs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the elevation of phosphorylated tau and Aβ (specified as Aβ1-42) with the same result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High levels of neuron- and glia-specific factors are detected in control and AD EVs, respectively. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this specific claim about neuron- vs glia-specific factor distributions; that preprint result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning identified ANXA5, VGF, GPM6A and ACTZ in AD EV compared to controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning identified ANXA5, VGF, GPM6A, and ACTZ in AD EV compared to controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the ML-identified protein set verbatim and at the same confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified proteins distinguished AD EVs from controls in the test sets with 88% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning distinguished AD EVs from controls in the test sets with 88% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The accuracy result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ANXA5, VGF, GPM6A and ACTZ are new signature proteins in AD EVs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ANXA5, VGF, GPM6A, and ACTZ are new signature proteins in AD EVs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same conclusion but with stronger certainty (medium→high), amplifying confidence in these proteins as signatures.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Raman spectroscopy and machine learning can accurately identify distinct metastatic phenotypes in tumors formed by isogenic murine breast cancer cell lines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Raman spectroscopy can accurately identify distinct metastatic phenotypes in tumors formed by isogenic murine breast cancer cell lines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in content and confidence to the preprint claim, only dropping the explicit mention of machine learning in wording while retaining the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models built on spectral data allow for the accurate identification of metastatic phenotype in independent test tumors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models built on spectral data can allow the accurate identification of metastatic phenotype of independent test tumors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is the same in substance and certainty; the publication restates the preprint result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Silencing genes critical for metastasis in highly metastatic cell lines results in predictions consistent with a phenotypic switch towards lower metastatic potential. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Silencing genes critical for metastasis in highly metastatic cell lines results in a phenotypic switch towards lower metastatic potential. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a stronger statement (an observed phenotypic switch) versus the preprint's phrasing about predictions consistent with a switch, so the claim is strengthened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spectral assessment of lipid and collagen content in tumors is consistent with the observed phenotypic switch. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spectral assessment of lipid and collagen content in tumors is consistent with the observed phenotypic switch. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint result with no substantive change in wording or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Raman spectroscopy may offer a novel strategy to evaluate metastatic risk during primary tumor biopsies in clinical patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Raman spectroscopy may offer a novel strategy to evaluate metastatic risk during primary tumor biopsies in clinical patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding, cautious clinical implication is restated in the publication without change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nearly all AUG uORFs were robust repressors of mRNA translation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nearly all AUG uORFs were robust repressors of gene expression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens wording from repression of mRNA translation to repression of gene expression (a wider concept), representing a broadened scope while preserving the underlying result and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most non-AUG uORFs had relatively weak impacts on expression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most non-AUG uORFs had relatively weak impacts on expression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: uORF functions are strongly impacted by both their sequences and locations within transcript leaders. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: uORF sequences and locations within transcript leaders predict their effect on gene expression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the relationship in predictive terms, clarifying how sequence and location relate to effects on expression while preserving the same causal link.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Alternative transcription start sites highly influenced uORF activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Alternative transcription start sites highly influenced uORF activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The locations of uORFs in transcript leaders are nearly as important as uORF sequences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The locations of uORFs in transcript leaders are nearly as predictive as uORF sequences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes predictive value rather than the more general term 'important,' clarifying the nature of the comparison without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: immuneML addresses concerns of reproducibility, transparency, and interoperability in AIRR ML. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: immuneML addresses concerns of reproducibility, transparency, and interoperability in AIRR ML. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim with no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: immuneML is available as a command-line tool and through an intuitive Galaxy web interface. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: immuneML is available as a command-line tool and through an intuitive Galaxy web interface. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Availability and interface description are unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The broad applicability of immuneML is demonstrated by reproducing a large-scale study on immune state prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The broad applicability of immuneML is demonstrated by reproducing a large-scale study on immune state prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the reproducibility demonstration without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel method for antigen specificity prediction has been developed, integrated, and applied within immuneML. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel deep learning method for antigen specificity prediction has been developed and integrated into immuneML. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the novel method is a deep learning approach, clarifying the technique used while preserving the claim's substance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Streamlined interpretability-focused benchmarking of AIRR ML is showcased using immuneML. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Streamlined interpretability-focused benchmarking of AIRR ML is showcased through immuneML. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the benchmarking and interpretability claim with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New methods out-performed the 2016 Truth Challenge winners. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not contain a corresponding claim that explicitly states new methods outperformed the 2016 winners; this specific comparative claim appears omitted in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Graph-based and machine-learning methods scored best for short-read and long-read datasets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Graph-based and machine learning methods scored best for short-read and long-read datasets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance result with identical substance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent developments in sequencing and variant calling have enabled benchmarking variants in challenging genomic regions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent developments in sequencing and variant calling have enabled benchmarking variants in challenging genomic regions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is present verbatim but the publication expresses higher certainty, strengthening the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New machine-learning approaches combining multiple sequencing technologies performed particularly well. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining multiple sequencing technologies with machine learning approaches performed particularly well. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same finding but assigns a lower certainty (medium vs high), indicating a tempered confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The challenge aimed to assess the state-of-the-art of variant calling in difficult-to-map regions and the Major Histocompatibility Complex (MHC). (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The precisionFDA Truth Challenge V2 assessed the state of the art of variant calling in challenging genomic regions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the goal of assessing variant calling in challenging regions (omitting explicit mention of the MHC), thereby narrowing the stated scope while increasing confidence in the assessment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reveals distinct decision-making components for bacterial growth among various growth phases, specifically glucose, sulfate, and serine for maximum growth, growth rate, and growth delay, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Glucose, sulfate, and serine are critical for maximum growth, growth rate, and growth delay, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's specific nutrient-function mapping matches the preprint's reported decision-making components and metrics directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Branched-chain amino acids function as global coordinators for population dynamics in Escherichia coli. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Branched-chain amino acids functioned as global coordinators for population dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same conclusion about branched-chain amino acids coordinating population dynamics with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that branched-chain amino acids serve as a survival strategy of risk diversification to prevent bacterial population extinction. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Branched-chain amino acids serve as a survival strategy of risk diversification to prevent bacterial population extinction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same evolutionary/strategic interpretation but expresses it with higher certainty (medium vs low), strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantile and Training Distribution Matching normalization allow for supervised and unsupervised model training on microarray and RNA-seq data simultaneously. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantile and Training Distribution Matching normalization allow for supervised and unsupervised model training on microarray and RNA-seq data simultaneously. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result verbatim, so the claim is unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonparanormal normalization and z-scores are also appropriate for some applications, including differential expression analysis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonparanormal normalization and z-scores are appropriate for some applications, including pathway analysis with Pathway-Level Information Extractor (PLIER). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same overall point (these normalizations are appropriate for some applications) but replaces the example application, clarifying the intended use case without changing the claim's scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data structure and distributions between microarray and RNA-seq platforms differ, making it challenging to combine them. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to perform effective cross-platform normalization using existing methods to combine microarray and RNA-seq data for machine learning applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized difficulty combining platforms, whereas the publication asserts that existing methods can effectively normalize and combine them; this reverses/tightens the conclusion and presents a more positive, broader claim with higher confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed smartphone-based mobile PPG and thermal imaging system can provide a fast binary measure of stress responses within 20 seconds of an event finishing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate the feasibility of using smartphone-based imaging for instant stress detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core conclusion (feasibility of instant stress detection using smartphone imaging) but omits the specific 20-second timing, making the claim slightly less specific/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The measured cardiac signals were considered highly reliable with a mean signal goodness probability of 0.9584. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The measured PPG signals presented high quality cardiac cyclic information with a mean pSQI of 0.755. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims report high-quality cardiac signals, but the publication uses a different quality metric (pSQI) with a lower reported mean, so the quantitative characterization has been revised and somewhat tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of HRV and thermal modalities produced a higher accuracy of 76.96% in binary classification tasks compared to using HRV or thermal features alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining both PPG and thermal imaging modalities produced higher accuracy in stress detection than using either modality alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the qualitative result (combining modalities improves accuracy) but omits the exact percentage, so the claim is clarified (less numerically specific) while retaining scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate that smartphone biomedical imaging can effectively recognize instant mental stress without the need for long measurements. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate the feasibility of using smartphone-based imaging for instant stress detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates feasibility of instant stress detection using smartphone imaging; wording is similar though the publication expresses this with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pores do not form homogeneously across the membrane, but colocalize with domains that have specific features, particularly a high density of polyunsaturated lipids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pores do not form homogeneously across the membrane, but colocalize with domains that have specific features, particularly a high density of polyunsaturated lipids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim; there is no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Knowing the lipid organization is sufficient to reliably predict poration sites with machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Knowing the lipid organization is sufficient to reliably predict poration sites with machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated identically in the publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Poration does not depend solely on local lipid arrangement, but also on membrane mechanical properties and the polarity of the electric field. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Poration does not depend solely on local lipid arrangement, but also on membrane mechanical properties and the polarity of the electric field. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same multifactorial result without substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of atomistic and coarse-grained molecular dynamics simulations, machine learning methods, and Bayesian survival analysis can guide the design of future experiments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of atomistic and coarse-grained molecular dynamics simulations, machine learning methods, and Bayesian survival analysis can guide the design of future experiments. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The textual claim is the same; only the label changed from 'conclusion' to 'contribution', so the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetaScore consistently outperforms each of nine traditional scoring functions in terms of success rate and hit rate evaluated over the top 10 predicted conformations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetaScore consistently outperforms each of the nine traditional scoring functions included in this work in terms of success rate and hit rate evaluated over conformations ranked among the top 10. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative comparative result with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An ensemble method, MetaScore-Ensemble, that combines 10 variants of MetaScore outperforms each of the MetaScore variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An ensemble method, MetaScore-Ensemble, that combines 10 variants of MetaScore obtained by combining the RF score with each of the traditional scoring functions outperforms each of the MetaScore variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides additional detail about how the ensemble variants were constructed (RF combined with each scoring function), clarifying the original ensemble claim without changing its outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of traditional scoring functions can be improved upon by judiciously leveraging machine-learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of traditional scoring functions can be improved upon by using machine learning to judiciously leverage protein-protein interfacial features. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the conclusion by specifying that protein-protein interfacial features are the relevant inputs for machine learning, clarifying rather than changing the substantive point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning model increases prediction accuracy for translation initiation sites by capturing the effect of critical nucleotides through feature reduction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning models developed in this study achieve an accuracy of approximately 85-88%, surpassing the accuracy of previously published models by more than 18%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the achieved accuracy improvement but does not describe the claimed mechanistic explanation (feature reduction capturing critical nucleotides), so the mechanistic aspect is revised/omitted while the outcome remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new models achieve 85.00-87.79% accuracy, exceeding the accuracy of previously published models by more than 18%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning models developed in this study achieve an accuracy of approximately 85-88%, surpassing the accuracy of previously published models by more than 18%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports essentially the same accuracy range and comparative improvement, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models are used to identify translation initiation sites in genes associated with neurologic repeat expansion disorders, confirming previously discovered sites and predicting new ones. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models are utilized to identify translation initiation sites in genes linked to various neurologic repeat expansion disorders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the application of the models to those genes and presents it with higher certainty; detailed sub-results (confirmation and novel predictions) are reported separately in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We transformed images of strawberry fruit into human-recognizable categories using unsupervised machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We transformed images of strawberry fruit into human-recognizable categories using unsupervised machine learning and discovered 4 principal shape categories.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim restates the preprint transformation claim and adds the discovery of four categories in the same sentence, so the core claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We discovered four principal shape categories and inferred progression using the principal progression of k clusters (PPKC).
alignments.pub_claim_text: We transformed images of strawberry fruit into human-recognizable categories using unsupervised machine learning and discovered 4 principal shape categories.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the discovery of four categories but separates or downplays the PPKC progression detail across claims; the main discovery is kept but the methodological/progression emphasis is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classification accuracy ranged from 68.9 – 99.3% for the newly created, genetically correlated phenotypic variables describing a shape.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim reports the stated accuracy range or the specific accuracy results for the created phenotypic variables, so this quantitative result is absent in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrated that strawberry fruit shapes could be robustly quantified, accurately classified, and empirically ordered using image analyses, machine learning, and PPKC.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results demonstrated that strawberry fruit shapes could be robustly quantified, accurately classified, and empirically ordered using image analyses, machine learning, and PPKC.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim, indicating no semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods and approaches we applied in strawberry should apply to other fruits, vegetables, and specialty crops.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a claim about generalizing the methods to other crops, so this broader applicability statement is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A computational method was developed that combines sequence, structure, and dynamics-based molecular properties of GPCRs to predict thermostable mutations ahead of experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A computational method was developed that combines sequence-, structure-, and dynamics-based molecular properties of GPCRs to predict thermostable mutations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the development of the same integrative computational method with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method was trained on thermostability data for 1231 mutants, which is the largest publicly available dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method was trained on thermostability data for 1231 mutants, which is the largest publicly available data set.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the dataset size and relative novelty claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A blind prediction for thermostable mutations of the Complement factor C5a Receptor retrieved 36% of the thermostable mutants in the top 50 prioritized mutants compared to 3% in the first 50 attempts using systematic alanine scanning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A blind prediction for thermostable mutations of the complement factor C5a receptor 1 retrieved 36% of the thermostable mutants in the top 50 prioritized mutants.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the comparative result into separate statements (the 36% retrieval is explicit), separating out the alanine-scanning 3% figure which appears as a distinct publication claim; the core blind-prediction performance is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work represents a significant advancement in the development, validation, and testing of a computational framework that can be extended to other class A GPCRs and helical membrane proteins.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim the stated broad extension to other class A GPCRs and helical membrane proteins, so the forward-generalization statement is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning classifiers can estimate the relative 'nativeness' of RNA-ligand poses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning classifiers can estimate the relative 'nativeness' of RNA-ligand poses.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result directly with no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ranking poses based on classification scores from machine learning classifiers recovers native-like poses better than ranking based on docking scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ranking poses based on classification scores from machine learning classifiers recovers native-like poses better than ranking based on docking scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative ranking result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One of the classifiers could recover poses within 2.5 Å of the native poses in approximately 80% of the cases examined.
alignments.pub_claim_text: One of the classifiers could recover poses within 2.5 Å of the native poses in approximately 80% of the cases examined.
alignments.rationale: The performance statistic is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RNAPosers tool is made available to the academic community for RNA-ligand pose prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNAPosers tool is made available to the academic community for RNA-ligand pose prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same contribution claim about tool availability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach improves the spatial resolution of satellite soil moisture information from 27km to 1km grids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach improves the spatial resolution of satellite soil moisture information from 27km to 1km grids.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution of improving spatial resolution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The explained variance for all models-years was greater than 70% based on 10-fold cross-validation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The explained variance for all models-years was greater than 70% based on 10-fold cross-validation.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the cross-validation explained-variance claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 1km soil moisture grids showed higher correlations with field soil moisture observations than the original satellite soil moisture estimates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 1km soil moisture grids showed higher correlations with field soil moisture observations, improving from r2 = 0.1 to r2 = 0.46.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides numerical specifics (r2 values) that clarify and quantify the preprint's qualitative improvement claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fusion of geomorphometry methods and satellite soil moisture estimates increases the spatial resolution and accuracy of satellite-derived soil moisture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fusion of geomorphometry methods and satellite soil moisture estimates increases the spatial resolution and accuracy of satellite-derived soil moisture.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-level conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach can be applied to other satellite-derived soil moisture estimates and regions across the world.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about global or other-product generalizability, so the broader applicability statement is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Highly predictive classification models emerged that distinguished MDD Placebo from HC Placebo with an AUC of 0.87.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Highly predictive classification models can distinguish MDD participants from healthy controls and from those receiving amisulpride.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a broader, less numerically specific statement (drops the explicit AUC), broadening the comparison context but tempering the specific performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification model distinguished MDD Placebo from MDD Amisulpride with an AUC of 0.89.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Highly predictive classification models can distinguish MDD participants from healthy controls and from those receiving amisulpride.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core ability to distinguish those on amisulpride but presents it in broader terms and omits the specific AUC, so the claim is clarified and broadened while the numerical confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhanced dopaminergic signaling restores abnormal activation and connectivity in a widespread network of regions in MDD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Enhanced dopaminergic signaling restores abnormal activation and connectivity in a widespread network of regions in individuals with MDD.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the mechanism claim with higher stated certainty (publication label is higher), so the semantic content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide new insights into the pathophysiology of MDD and the pharmacological mechanism of antidepressants at the system level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings provide new insights into the pathophysiology of MDD and the pharmacological mechanisms of antidepressants.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same interpretive claim about insights into pathophysiology and mechanisms with similar framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Self-organized patterns can be used for distributed information encoding and decoding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-organized patterns combined with machine learning can achieve distributed information encoding and decoding.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the role of machine learning to the original claim, clarifying the implementation while keeping the core claim the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different groups of patterns arising from different initial configurations can be distinguished from one another for sufficiently small randomness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different groups of patterns arising from different initial configurations can be distinguished from one another for sufficiently small randomness.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed strategy is applicable to non-biological dynamical systems.
alignments.rationale: The final paper does not mention applicability to non-biological dynamical systems; that claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The encoding and decoding scheme can generate a complete mapping of the standard English keyboard, enabling the encoding of English text.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed strategy is scalable for encoding and decoding all characters of the standard English keyboard.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same capability emphasizing scalability and explicitly states encoding/decoding of all keyboard characters, clarifying the original mapping claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a way to simulate dynamic metabolomics data with a known ground truth based on ordinary differential equations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method to simulate dynamic metabolomics data with a known ground truth based on ordinary differential equations has been developed and is available through the MetaboLouise R package.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the simulation method claim and adds that the implementation is available via the MetaboLouise R package, clarifying accessibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The EDGE tool is highly performant in analyzing dynamic case vs control metabolomics data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The EDGE tool, originally developed for genomics data analysis, is highly performant in analyzing dynamic case vs. control metabolomics data.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates EDGE's high performance and adds provenance (developed for genomics), a clarifying detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tinderesting method performs on par with statistical methods for analyzing complex dynamic metabolomics experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tinderesting method is introduced to analyze more complex dynamic metabolomics experiments and utilizes a Shiny app for collecting expert knowledge to train a machine learning model.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes tinderesting as a new method and tool (with a Shiny app and ML training) and omits the explicit equivalence-to-statistical-methods performance claim, changing the emphasis and tempering performance assertions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tinderesting tool provides additional information, improved performance, or easier interpretation of results in metabolomics analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tinderesting approach does not replace traditional data analysis workflows for metabolomics but can provide additional information, improved performance, or easier interpretation of results.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the claimed benefits but adds the caveat that the approach complements rather than replaces traditional workflows, clarifying its intended role.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental evidence demonstrates that functional interactions organize into putative brain systems that facilitate different facets of cognitive computation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental evidence demonstrates that functional interactions organize into putative brain systems that facilitate different facets of cognitive computation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this experimental result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We find that subgraphs are stratified based on both the underlying modular organization and the topographical distance of their strongest interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We find that subgraphs are stratified based on both the underlying modular organization and the topographical distance of their strongest interactions.
alignments.rationale: The claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relationship between dynamic subgraphs and modular architecture is further highlighted by the ability of time-varying subgraph expression to explain inter-individual differences in module reorganization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The relationship between dynamic subgraphs and modular architecture is further highlighted by the ability of time-varying subgraph expression to explain inter-individual differences in module reorganization.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This machine learning approach opens a new door for understanding the architecture of dynamic functional networks during both task and rest states.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This machine learning approach opens a new door for understanding the architecture of dynamic functional networks during both task and rest states.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NERVE 2.0 shows higher predictive performance than its previous version and other web-RV programs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NERVE 2.0 shows higher predictive performance than its previous version and other web-RV programs.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the performance comparison claim as stated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NERVE 2.0 includes innovative modules such as Loop Razor and epitopepredict for improved vaccine candidate analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NERVE 2.0 includes innovative modules such as Loop-Razor and Epitope Prediction for improved vaccine candidate analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the same modules with minor naming/formatting differences but the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The user interface of NERVE 2.0 has been redesigned to be more user-friendly and accessible.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The user interface of NERVE 2.0 has been redesigned to be more user-friendly and accessible to users without bioinformatic skills.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the target audience (users without bioinformatic skills), strengthening the accessibility claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NERVE 2.0 allows customizable and refinable bacterial protein vaccine analyses for various users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NERVE 2.0 allows customizable and refinable bacterial protein vaccine analyses for various types of users.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the customizable analyses and frames the claim with higher certainty (confidence increased), amplifying the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Advanced Complex in vitro models (CIVMs) like Blood-brain barrier (BBB) organoids show promise for preclinical drug testing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of advanced morphology-based readouts and digital pathology algorithms is highlighted as a potential standard in preclinical drug development assessments using CIVMs.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the general 'promise' of CIVMs into a broader recommendation for integrating readouts and algorithms as a potential standard, but with somewhat lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Robust and reproducible techniques are crucial for the acceptance of CIVMs in drug development processes, especially for toxicity assessments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of advanced morphology-based readouts and digital pathology algorithms is highlighted as a potential standard in preclinical drug development assessments using CIVMs.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state the normative claim about "crucial" robustness; instead it highlights integration as a potential standard, which revises the original emphasis and is expressed with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed orthogonal image-based readouts on histological sections to enable the use of BBB organoids for future compound toxicity assessment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed set of orthogonal image-based tissue methods validates Complex in vitro models for drug toxicity assessments.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the contribution by stating the developed methods validate CIVMs for toxicity assessments, amplifying the practical validation beyond the preprint's enabling claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A newly established artificial intelligence (AI) algorithm provides automated and label-free detection of apoptotic cells in drug screening using BBB organoids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An artificial intelligence algorithm was developed to increase the throughput and reliability of histomorphologic evaluations of apoptosis for in vitro toxicity studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the AI algorithm claim while emphasizing throughput and reliability in histomorphologic apoptosis evaluation; this clarifies the algorithm's benefits without changing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Swarm learning (SL) successfully enables the training of AI models on large, multicentric datasets of gigapixel histopathology images comprising over 5000 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Swarm learning successfully enables the training of AI models on large, multicentric datasets of gigapixel histopathology images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core capability but omits the explicit 'over 5000 patients' sample-size detail, making the claim slightly less specific (clarified/narrowed) while retaining confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI models trained using Swarm Learning can predict BRAF mutational status and microsatellite instability (MSI) directly from hematoxylin and eosin (H&E)-stained pathology slides of colorectal cancer (CRC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI models trained using swarm learning can predict BRAF mutational status and microsatellite instability from H&E-stained pathology slides of colorectal cancer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SL-based AI models outperform most locally trained models and perform on par with models which are centrally trained on merged datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SL-trained AI models outperform most locally trained models and perform on par with models trained on merged datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is essentially identical and the performance comparison is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SL-based AI models are data efficient and maintain robust performance even when only subsets of local datasets are used for training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Swarm learning-based AI models are data efficient and can be used for any histopathology image analysis task without the need for data transfer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the preprint's specific data-efficiency finding into a general claim about applicability to any histopathology task and removes the explicit robustness-with-subsets phrasing, increasing generality and apparent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the future, SL can be used to train distributed AI models for any histopathology image analysis tasks, overcoming the need for data transfer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Swarm learning-based AI models are data efficient and can be used for any histopathology image analysis task without the need for data transfer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed this as a future possibility; the publication states the applicability more directly, effectively strengthening/broadening the conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial intelligence can predict the presence of molecular alterations directly from routine histopathology slides. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This general claim about AI predicting molecular alterations from histopathology slides does not appear among the specific preprint claims and is therefore an added, broader publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SVM weights reveal a set of distinct features as well as a set of shared features for the two executive functions updating and inhibition in the theta and the alpha band power. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SVM weights reveal a set of distinct features as well as a set of shared features for the two executive functions updating and inhibition in the theta and the alpha band power. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is identical between preprint and publication; claim content and strength are preserved.
