alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper finds evidence that correlates for unity and diversity of executive functions can be found in neurophysiological data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper finds evidence that correlates for unity and diversity of executive functions can be found in neurophysiological data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and implication remain the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning approaches reveal shared but also distinct properties for the executive functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning approaches reveal shared but also distinct properties for the executive functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No substantive change between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using methods from brain-computer interface research, like machine learning, as a tool for the validation of psychological models is a new approach that is highly versatile. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using methods from brain-computer interface research, like machine learning, as a tool for the validation of psychological models is a new approach that is highly versatile. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology developed allows for the visualization and quantitative analysis of the rhizoid system of the moss, Physcomitrium patens, in 3D. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology developed allows for the visualization and quantitative analysis of the rhizoid system of the moss, Physcomitrium patens, in 3D. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the methodological contribution exactly, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automatic segmentation of rhizoids using machine learning was found to be more effective than using the Canny edge detector. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automatic segmentation using machine learning was found to be more effective than using the Canny edge detector for analyzing rhizoids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved in meaning and confidence, with only minor rewording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning-based skeletonized 3D model revealed a dense distribution of rhizoids similar to the root system architecture in vascular plants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning-based skeletonized 3D model revealed a quite dense distribution of rhizoids. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the explicit comparison to vascular plant root architecture and tones the language to a more modest 'quite dense distribution,' reducing scope and strength of the original comparative conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study successfully visualized the moss rhizoid system in 3D for the first time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study successfully visualized the moss rhizoid system in 3D for the first time. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The 'first visualization in 3D' claim is maintained with equivalent framing and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Three types of shape (ring-shape, line, and black circle) observed in tomographic slices were confirmed to be rhizoids by optical and electron microscopy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a specific microscopy-confirmation result (three shape types) that was not present in the preprint claims, so this is an added published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine Learning techniques can be effectively used for multivariate analysis in studies of motor control. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine Learning techniques can be effectively used for multivariate analysis in studies of motor control. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA) allows for successful binary classifications of EMG vectors based on pointing angles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA) allows for successful binary classification of EMG vectors based on pointing angles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result is preserved with only trivial wording change ('classification' vs 'classifications').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Post classification feature selection can identify significant combinations of features altered between task conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Post classification feature selection can identify significant combinations of features altered between task conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The methodological conclusion remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach quantifies the effects of entire EMG patterns rather than relying on single arbitrarily defined variables. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach quantifies the effects of entire EMG patterns rather than relying on single arbitrarily defined variables. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No substantive change in claim content or strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method identifies parts of EMG patterns that convey the most information regarding the investigated effects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method identifies parts of EMG patterns that convey the most information regarding the investigated effects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implantation outcome is correlated with attributes derived from the cohort siblings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implantation outcome is correlated with attributes derived from the cohort siblings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unlabeled data from sibling embryos boosts implantation prediction performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unlabeled data from sibling embryos boosts implantation prediction performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance-improvement claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cohort properties can rescue erroneous predictions in embryo implantation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cohort properties driving embryo prediction, especially those that rescued erroneous predictions, are characterized. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from announcing that cohort properties can rescue errors to characterizing the cohort properties that performed that function, clarifying rather than contradicting the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive models for embryo implantation can benefit from the unlabeled data of sibling embryos. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive models for embryo implantation can benefit from the overlooked, widely available unlabeled data of sibling embryos. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but emphasizes the overlooked and widely available nature of the unlabeled sibling data, a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating cohort features in AI-driven embryo implantation prediction can have direct translational implications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive models for embryo implantation can benefit from the overlooked, widely available unlabeled data of sibling embryos. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more measured claim about benefit from unlabeled sibling data and omits the explicit statement about 'direct translational implications,' thereby tempering and narrowing the original translational assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GRU-SCANET achieves high precision in entity labeling by combining positional encoding, bidirectional GRUs, an attention-based encoder, and a conditional random field decoder. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GRU-SCANET achieves high precision in entity labeling by combining positional encoding, bidirectional GRUs, an attention-based encoder, and a conditional random field decoder. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is word-for-word the same as the preprint claim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GRU-SCANET consistently outperforms leading benchmarks, achieving better performance than BioBERT, PubMedBERT, and previous state-of-the-art models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GRU-SCANET consistently outperforms leading benchmarks, achieving better performance than BioBERT, PubMedBERT, and previous state-of-the-art models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design of GRU-SCANET effectively mitigates the challenges posed by unbalanced data across multiple corpora. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design of GRU-SCANET effectively mitigates the challenges posed by unbalanced data across multiple corpora. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this methodological/conclusion claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GRU-SCANET offers a computationally efficient alternative for extracting biological entities by capturing contextual dependencies within biomedical texts. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GRU-SCANET captures token-entity relationships more effectively than existing methods, advancing the state of biomedical named entity recognition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from computational efficiency to improved token-entity relationship modeling and advancement of NER, which revises and broadens the original contribution and reads as a stronger claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HIVE can recover the true, underlying inter-session variability and show improved performance over existing approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIVE can uncover true subpopulation structure and show improved performance over existing approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes 'inter-session variability' as 'subpopulation structure,' changing the target of the claim; performance improvement remains, so confidence is unchanged but the claim content is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learnt embedding vectors (session fingerprints) reflect meaningful sources of variation across a population. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learnt embedding vectors (session fingerprints) reflect meaningful sources of variation across a population. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim directly with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HIVE provides a powerful new technique for modelling individual sessions while leveraging information available across an entire group. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIVE provides a powerful new approach for modelling individual sessions while leveraging information available across an entire group. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this methodological contribution with equivalent meaning (technique vs approach is wording only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-omic analysis can define generalizable microbial processes and is especially useful in understanding complex conditions such as Autism. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-omic analysis can define generalizable microbial processes and is especially useful in understanding complex conditions such as Autism. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify metabolites, specifically neurotransmitter precursors and fatty acid derivatives, that differ significantly between children with and without Autism. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify metabolites, specifically neurotransmitter precursors and fatty acid derivatives, that differ significantly between children with and without Autism. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported differential metabolites claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify clusters of topics, deemed 'cross-omic topics', which we hypothesize are representative of generalizable microbial processes observable regardless of profiling method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify clusters of topics, deemed 'cross-omic topics', which we hypothesize are representative of generalizable microbial processes observable regardless of profiling method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same cross-omic topics claim with matching wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each identified topic represents a particular diet, labeled as healthy/general function, age-associated function, transcriptional regulation, and opportunistic pathogenesis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each identified topic represents a particular diet, and we heuristically label each cross-omic topic as: healthy/general function, age-associated function, transcriptional regulation, and opportunistic pathogenesis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that the labels are heuristic (a qualification), which reduces the force of the original labeling claim and changes its framing from result to a more cautious conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our best models can perform a previously unseen task with a performance of 83% correct on average based solely on linguistic instructions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our best models can perform a previously unseen task with an average performance of 83% correct based solely on linguistic instructions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is reproduced exactly in the publication with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Language scaffolds sensorimotor representations such that activity for interrelated tasks share a common geometry with the semantic representations of instructions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Language scaffolds sensorimotor representations such that activity for interrelated tasks shares a common geometry with the semantic representations of instructions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion is preserved (only minor grammatical adjustment).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can generate a linguistic description of a novel task it has identified using only motor feedback. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model generates a linguistic description of a novel task it has identified using only motor feedback. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same experimental result claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our models offer several experimentally testable predictions outlining how linguistic information must be represented in order to facilitate flexible and general cognition in the human brain. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models offer several experimentally testable predictions outlining how linguistic information must be represented to facilitate flexible and general cognition in the human brain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same contribution but increases stated certainty from medium to high, thus amplifying confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoCoScore outperforms previous approaches in identifying human disease-gene and tissue-gene associations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoCoScore outperforms previous approaches in identifying human disease-gene and tissue-gene associations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this performance claim directly (matched to Q1).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoCoScore also outperforms previous methods in identifying physical and functional protein-protein associations in different species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoCoScore also outperforms previous methods in identifying physical and functional protein-protein associations in different species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this cross-species protein–protein association performance claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoCoScore is trained using distant supervision based on a gold-standard set of associations between entities of interest. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention the training via distant supervision on a gold-standard associations set; that methodological detail appears omitted from the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoCoScore is a versatile text-mining tool to uncover pairwise associations via co-occurrence mining. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoCoScore is a versatile text mining tool that can uncover pairwise associations via co-occurrence mining in various applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the tool-versatility claim but clarifies/broadens it by noting applicability in various applications and frames it as a conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: CoCoScore is a novel corpus-wide co-occurrence scoring approach that takes the textual context of each co-mention into account. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an explicit claim of novelty and contextualized corpus-wide scoring that was not present in the preprint claims, so this is an added publication-level claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A quantitative systems pharmacology model was developed to represent TMZ cellular pharmacokinetics-pharmacodynamics and their connection to altered pathways in glioblastoma.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A QSP model representing TMZ cellular pharmacokinetics-pharmacodynamics and dysregulated pathways in GBM was developed and validated.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the model development but explicitly adds that the QSP model was validated, which clarifies and strengthens the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In silico target inhibition screening identified an optimal antitumor strategy that involves priming cancer cells with inhibitors of the base excision repair and homologous recombination pathway prior to TMZ exposure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In silico drug screening and subsequent experimental validation identified a strategy to re-sensitise TMZ-resistant cells by combining TMZ with inhibitors of the base excision repair and of homologous recombination.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same strategy and adds that experimental validation confirmed re-sensitisation, clarifying and strengthening the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The drug combination identified was validated in experiments, allowing re-sensitization of cells initially resistant to TMZ.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In silico drug screening and subsequent experimental validation identified a strategy to re-sensitise TMZ-resistant cells by combining TMZ with inhibitors of the base excision repair and of homologous recombination.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report experimental validation of the identified drug combination and the resulting re-sensitisation of resistant cells with closely matching scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning was used to derive functional signatures of response to the optimal multiagent therapy to assist in decision making for other cancer cell lines or patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We successfully demonstrated the relevance of combined QSP and ML to design efficient drug combinations re-sensitising glioblastoma cells initially resistant to TMZ.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses the ML contribution on demonstrating relevance of QSP+ML to design re-sensitising drug combinations (narrower than assisting decision-making across lines/patients) and reports the result with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed framework can be extended to account for patient-specific altered pathways and may be translated towards clinical applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed framework may further serve to identify personalised therapies and administration schedules by extending it to account for additional patient-specific altered pathways and whole-body features.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands and clarifies potential extensions (personalised therapies, schedules, whole-body features), broadening the original translational suggestion while keeping similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed culture-free workflow enables bacterial identification and quantification in less than 4 hours using 1mL of urine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed culture-free workflow enables bacterial identification and quantification in less than 4 hours using 1 ml of urine.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates 99.2% accuracy on bacterial inoculations of healthy urines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieved 99.2% accuracy on bacterial inoculations of healthy urines.
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance claim is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The workflow was validated on 45 inoculated urines and on 84 UTI-positive urine from patients, achieving 93.3% and 87.1% agreement with the culture-MALDI procedure, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The workflow was validated on 45 inoculated urines and on 84 UTI-positive urine from patients, showing 93.3% and 87.1% agreement with the culture-MALDI procedure, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same validation data and agreement metrics as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can quantify the spread of the infection from 8×10^4 to 3×10^7 CFU/mL.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can quantify the spread of the infection from 8 × 10^4 to 3 × 10^7 CFU/ml.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative detection range is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature selection based on biological previous knowledge improves the performances of classifiers on metabolomics datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying supervised feature selection improves the performances of feature extraction methods for classification purposes across all datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalises the preprint's metabolomics- and biologically-guided feature-selection claim to supervised selection improving feature-extraction performance across datasets, representing a shift in focus and broader scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metabolomics has emerged as a powerful tool in cancer research, aiding in the understanding of the metabolic landscape associated with tumorigenesis and tumor progression.
alignments.rationale: This background statement about metabolomics in cancer does not have a corresponding claim in the publication's listed claims and appears omitted from the core claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides general workflow and guidelines on the use of feature extraction and selection techniques depending on the characteristics of the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides general workflow and guidelines on when to use feature extraction and selection techniques based on the characteristics of the data available.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the workflow and guideline contribution with essentially identical wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A mean of 1,225 proteins were quantified in the control group, while 915 proteins were quantified in the case group.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A mean of 1,225 proteins were quantified in the control group, while 915 proteins were quantified in the case group.
alignments.rationale: The numeric proteomics quantification is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three hundred and thirty-two differentially abundant proteins were constructed into a network associated with pathways including the immune system and extracellular matrix organisation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three hundred and thirty-two differentially abundant proteins were identified and constructed into a network.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same set of differentially abundant proteins and network construction; the pathway detail appears in a separate claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proteins with high disease scores for both hypertension and chronic kidney disease included angiotensinogen, albumin, apolipoprotein L1, and uromodulin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pathways associated with the differentially abundant proteins included the immune system, innate immune system, extracellular matrix organisation, and activation of matrix metalloproteinases.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the pathway annotation beyond the preprint's phrasing (adding innate immune system and MMP activation), clarifying and broadening the pathway-level claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proteins with high disease scores for both hypertension and chronic kidney disease included angiotensinogen, albumin, apolipoprotein L1, and uromodulin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proteins with high disease scores for both hypertension and chronic kidney disease included angiotensinogen, albumin, apolipoprotein L1, and uromodulin.
alignments.rationale: The specific proteins identified with high disease scores are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The urinary proteomic data combined with the machine learning approach was able to classify disease status and identify proteins and pathways associated with hypertension and albuminuria.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The urinary proteomic data combined with the machine learning approach was able to classify disease status and identify proteins and pathways associated with hypertension-associated albuminuria.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the association as 'hypertension-associated albuminuria', which narrows and clarifies the disease context of the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models were built that predict compound activity against asexual blood stage (ABS) and dual-activity against ABS and gametocytes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models were built that predict compound activity against asexual blood stage (ABS) and dual activity against ABS and gametocytes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core modeling contribution with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Support Vector Machines (SVM) demonstrated superior abilities with high recall rates of 90% and 66% and low false positive predictions of 15% and 1%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Support Vector Machines (SVM) demonstrated superior abilities with high recall and low false-positive predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the specific numeric performance to a qualitative statement, which clarifies presentation but removes numeric specificity and thus tempers the expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive capabilities of the models are robust and can serve as a prioritization tool for phenotypic screening and medicinal chemistry programs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive capabilities of the models are robust and can serve as a prioritization tool for phenotypic screening and medicinal chemistry programs.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about model utility is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified chemical features enriched in active and inactive populations, which can streamline hit-to-lead optimization strategies of antimalarial candidates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data collected can help identify chemical features associated with both active and inactive compounds, potentially streamlining future screening processes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the finding as data that can help identify features and potentially streamline screening (a slightly more cautious phrasing), clarifying and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Expansions of tandem repeats (TRs) are responsible for approximately 50 monogenic diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Expansions of tandem repeats (TRs) cause approximately 60 monogenic diseases.
alignments.rationale: Both claims state the same phenomenon but the published version reports a different (larger) numerical estimate, representing a revision of the result and a stronger (amplified) numeric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RExPRT classifies TRs with a precision of 92% and a recall of 90% based on leave-one-out cross validation results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An ensemble approach classifies TRs with an average precision of 93% and recall of 83%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different performance numbers and describes an ensemble approach rather than the exact leave-one-out results reported in the preprint, lowering recall and thus tempering the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Application of RExPRT to ~800,000 TRs in the reference genome identified ~30,000 TRs that would be likely pathogenic upon expansion.
alignments.rationale: The specific large-scale application result (~800k TRs -> ~30k likely pathogenic) appears in the preprint but is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RExPRT establishes a foundation for the application of machine learning approaches to categorize the pathogenicity of tandem repeat loci.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RExPRT (Repeat EXpansion Pathogenicity pRediction Tool) is a machine learning tool for distinguishing pathogenic from benign TR expansions.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's broader foundational statement into a clearer contribution statement describing RExPRT's role as a specific ML tool for distinguishing pathogenic vs benign TRs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study achieved an accuracy of 91% in classifying non-classical secreted proteins using a Random Forest machine-learning algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieved an impressive accuracy rate of approximately 91% in classifying non-classical secreted proteins using a Random Forest machine learning algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same accuracy claim with essentially identical wording and context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensitivity and specificity of the classification were 91% and 86%, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The validation of our model resulted in a sensitivity of 91% and a specificity of 86% on a dataset of 14 positive and 92 negative proteins.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same sensitivity/specificity values but adds dataset-size detail, clarifying the validation context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for classifying non-classical secreted proteins is significantly faster than current methods, reducing classification time from dozens of seconds to just one second for a few dozen samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study distinguishes itself by its rapid execution of non-classical secretion pathway analyses, making it suitable for large datasets without compromising accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the rapid-execution contribution but frames it more generally as suitability for large datasets rather than reporting the specific timing numbers, broadening the claimed applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research demonstrates that selecting an appropriate set of descriptors and a comprehensive training dataset can compensate for not using advanced machine learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The careful selection of appropriate descriptors and the construction of a robust training dataset are significant for enhancing Omics analyses of bacterial protein secretion via non-classical pathways.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core insight about descriptor selection/training data but places it in a broader Omics analysis context, expanding the claimed relevance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Machine Learning Cumulative Performance Score (MLcps) combines multiple pre-computed performance metrics into one metric.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MLcps integrates several precomputed evaluation metrics into a unified score, enabling a comprehensive assessment of the trained model's strengths and weaknesses.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same contribution: combining multiple precomputed metrics into one unified score; wording is slightly different but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MLcps provides a comprehensive picture of overall model robustness when tested on 4 different publicly available biological datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MLcps provides a holistic evaluation of the model's robustness, ensuring a thorough understanding of its overall performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim that MLcps assesses robustness but omits the explicit mention of the four specific datasets, making the claim more general/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The selection of a performance metric for ML models becomes particularly challenging in clinical studies with imbalanced and small datasets.
alignments.rationale: This preprint observation about challenges in clinical/imbalanced small datasets does not appear in the published claims and is not represented there.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comparison of multiple performance metrics is laborious and prone to user preference bias.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing MLcps allows researchers and practitioners to rely on a single value to assess the overall performance of their ML models.
alignments.rationale: The preprint highlights a limitation of comparing many metrics; the publication reframes this into a positive claim that MLcps provides a single-value solution, revising the limitation into a claimed benefit and broadening the practical implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biofilm production significantly contributes to the pathogenicity of many bacteria and limits the effectiveness of antimicrobial agents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biofilm production significantly contributes to the pathogenicity of many bacteria and limits the effectiveness of antimicrobial agents.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bacteriophage depolymerases can degrade the extracellular matrix of biofilms, potentially enhancing the effectiveness of therapies against antibiotic resistant bacteria.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bacteriophage depolymerases can degrade the extracellular matrix of biofilms, potentially enhancing the effectiveness of therapies against antibiotic resistant bacteria.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim and unchanged in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning based approach can identify phage depolymerases with a model accuracy of approximately 90% using a limited number of experimentally proven enzymes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning based approach can identify phage depolymerases with an accuracy of approximately 90% using a limited number of experimentally proven enzymes.
alignments.rationale: The reported accuracy and experimental setup are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of a powerful model for identifying phage depolymerases demonstrates the value of machine learning in discovering novel therapeutic agents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of a powerful model for identifying phage depolymerases demonstrates the value of machine learning in protein functional annotation.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the claimed broader therapeutic-discovery impact to a more specific statement about protein functional annotation, revising and narrowing the claimed application area.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MINT performed well across tasks, suggesting its assumptions are well-matched to the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MINT performed well across tasks, suggesting its assumptions are well-matched to the data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result with the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MINT outperformed other interpretable methods in every comparison we made.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MINT outperformed other interpretable methods in every comparison we made.
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MINT outperformed expressive machine learning methods in 37 of 42 comparisons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MINT outperformed expressive machine learning methods in 37 of 42 comparisons.
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and comparison are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MINT’s computations are simple, scale favorably with increasing neuron counts, and yield interpretable quantities such as data likelihoods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MINT’s computations are simple, scale favorably with increasing neuron counts, and yield interpretable quantities such as data likelihoods.
alignments.rationale: The methodological and interpretability claims are carried over without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MINT may be a strong candidate for many BCI applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MINT may be a strong candidate for many BCI applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same prospective application claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamic perturbation approach effectively manipulates the relative memory strength of a list of working memory items. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel 'dynamic perturbation' approach effectively manipulates the relative memory strength of working memory items held in the human brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P0 with essentially identical scope and confidence; wording differences are minimal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A computational model combining continuous attractor neural network and short-term synaptic plasticity principles reproduces all empirical findings related to working memory. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A computational model combining continuous attractor neural network and short-term synaptic plasticity principles reproduces all the empirical findings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 is a direct restatement of P1; both assert the same modeling result with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamic perturbation modifies the synaptic efficacies of working memory items through short-term synaptic plasticity principles. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 'dynamic perturbation' modifies the synaptic efficacies of working memory items through short-term synaptic plasticity principles. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q3 repeats P2's mechanistic claim nearly verbatim and retains the same confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results support the causal role of temporal dynamics of neural networks in mediating multi-item working memory. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results support the causal role of temporal dynamics of neural networks in mediating multi-item working memory. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q4 repeats the causal claim of P3 but reduces stated certainty in the publication, so the claim is preserved but tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study offers a promising, non-invasive approach to manipulate working memory. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly asserts that the method is a 'promising, non-invasive approach'; this contribution-language is absent from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cell death occurs continuously throughout insect neurogenesis and happens soon after neurons are born. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cell death occurs continuously throughout insect neurogenesis and happens soon after neurons are born. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 matches P0 exactly in content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reductions in populations of flight interneurons in dipterans are likely caused by increased cell death during development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reductions in populations of flight interneurons in two dipterans are likely caused by increased cell death during development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q3 preserves the causal conclusion of P1 but specifies it applies to two dipteran species, narrowing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Blocking programmed cell death in Drosophila melanogaster results in the production of 'undead' neurons with complex arborisations and distinct neurotransmitter identities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Blocking cell death in the medial neuroblast lineage of Drosophila melanogaster results in the production of 'undead' neurons with complex arborisations and distinct neurotransmitter identities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 restates P2 but adds the anatomical specificity (medial neuroblast lineage), clarifying and narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 'undead' neurons are functional as demonstrated by activation and recordings of neural activity in behaving animals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Activation of 'undead' neurons and recordings of neural activity in behaving animals demonstrate that they are functional. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 mirrors P3 directly, presenting the same experimental evidence and conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolutionary modulation of death-based patterning could generate novel network configurations. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolutionary modulation of death-based patterning could generate novel network configurations. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q4 repeats P4 verbatim, preserving speculative evolutionary interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep neural network architecture called Deeply Essential predicts essential genes in microbes using only gene primary sequence and corresponding protein sequence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeeplyEssential, a deep neural network, can predict essential genes in microbes using only gene primary sequence and corresponding protein sequence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P0's core contribution directly and with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deeply Essential outperforms existing classifiers that use down-sampling or clustering methods to balance the training set or exclude multiple copies of orthologous genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeeplyEssential outperforms existing classifiers that use down-sampling or clustering methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 conveys the same comparative performance claim as P1, with equivalent emphasis on outperforming existing classifiers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hidden performance bias that affected previous classifiers has been exposed and studied. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hidden performance bias affecting previous classifiers has been exposed and studied. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q2 matches P2 in content; wording and role (contribution vs conclusion) differ slightly but the substance is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model that includes only promoter sequences and features associated with mRNA stability explains 59% and 71% of variation in steady-state mRNA levels in human and mouse, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model that includes only promoter sequences and features associated with mRNA stability explains 59% and 71% of variation in steady-state mRNA levels in human and mouse, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 is effectively identical to P0 in numbers, inputs, and claimed explanatory power.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model Xpresso more than doubles the accuracy of alternative sequence-based models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model, termed Xpresso, more than doubles the accuracy of alternative sequence-based models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 restates P1 verbatim, so the claim is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Xpresso recapitulates genome-wide patterns of transcriptional activity and predicts the influence of enhancers, heterochromatic domains, and microRNAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Xpresso recapitulates genome-wide patterns of transcriptional activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 retains the broad recapitulation claim but omits the specific subclaims about enhancers, heterochromatin, and microRNAs, narrowing the original set of asserted predictive influences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model interpretation reveals that promoter-proximal CpG dinucleotides strongly predict transcriptional activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model interpretation reveals that promoter-proximal CpG dinucleotides strongly predict transcriptional activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q3 restates P3's finding but the publication labels it with somewhat lower certainty, so the claim is preserved but tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accurate prediction of cell type-specific gene expression based solely on primary sequence is proposed as a grand challenge for the field. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose cell-type-specific gene-expression predictions based solely on primary sequences as a grand challenge for the field. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q4 matches P4's proposal for a field-level challenge with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new approach to contact prediction can extract correlation information and predict contacts in a supervised manner directly from MSA using a deep neural network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The end-to-end learning framework can use information derived from either deep or shallow MSAs for contact prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q3 captures the same core idea (using MSAs in an end-to-end neural framework) but frames it as an end-to-end framework applicable to deep or shallow MSAs, clarifying the method's formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the deep neural network, higher accuracy can be obtained than with earlier ECA methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: More accurate three-dimensional models of a target protein are obtainable than those from existing ECA methods, starting from its MSA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q4 expresses an improvement over ECA methods that extends the implication to more accurate 3D models; the publication broadens the impact relative to the preprint's contact-accuracy framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can automatically weight each sequence in MSA to eliminate noise sequences in a supervised way. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher prediction accuracy was achieved by assigning weights to sequences in a deep MSA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 documents the same weighting-of-sequences approach; the publication presents it as achieving higher prediction accuracy and with higher certainty, amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of this method and other meta-learning methods is expected to provide much higher accuracy of contact prediction. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our final model is superior to or equivalent to existing meta-predictors based on tests using past CASP target protein domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: P3 was a low-certainty expectation about combining methods; the publication reports tested performance against meta-predictors, converting the speculative expectation into an empirical (and stronger) result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a sweet spot in training difficulty where learning progresses most quickly. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a sweet spot in training difficulty where learning progresses most quickly. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion verbatim, so the claim is unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.
