alignments.pub_claim_text: The Multimodal Variational Information Bottleneck (MVIB) model achieves competitive classification performance while being faster than existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same speed comparison as part of the combined performance-and-speed claim, so the core claim about being faster is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model provides interpretable results by identifying the most relevant microbial species and strain-level markers for predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model provides interpretable results by identifying the most relevant microbial species and strain-level markers to the model's predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the interpretability claim with essentially the same content but downgrades certainty (high->medium), so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MVIB is scalable with respect to input data modalities, allowing for the inclusion of metabolomic data as a third input. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MVIB is scalable with respect to input data modalities and has an average training time of less than 1.4 seconds. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the scalability claim and adds a concrete training-time measurement, clarifying and strengthening (amplifying) the original scalability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MVIB achieves high performance (0.80 < ROC AUC < 0.95) on 5 disease cohorts and at least medium performance on the remaining cohorts evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MVIB demonstrates high performance (0.80 < ROC AUC < 0.95) on 5 cohorts and at least medium performance on the remaining cohorts evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance metrics and cohort-level claim with the same certainty, so this is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed hybrid features enable prediction models to achieve more promising performance compared to seven prominent sequence-based feature types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid features improve prediction model performance compared to seven prominent sequence-based feature types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative improvement claim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On average, our optimal models achieve an area under the curve of 0.984 on 5-fold cross-validation and 0.998 on independent tests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal models achieve an area under the curve of 0.984 on 5-fold cross-validation and 0.998 on independent tests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the exact reported performance metrics and maintains the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biologists can efficiently use our model to identify tumor necrosis factors from other cytokines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biologists can efficiently identify tumor necrosis factors from other cytokines using the developed model. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same practical usability claim but increases the expressed certainty (medium->high), amplifying confidence in the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates that natural language processing techniques can be reasonably applied to help biologists solve bioinformatics problems efficiently. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Natural language processing techniques can be effectively applied to solve bioinformatics problems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same general conclusion about NLP applicability but expresses it with lower certainty (high->medium), so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose to use a recurrent neural network architecture based on long short-term memory networks (LSTM) to predict the time series of the actions taken by human subjects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed recurrent neural network architecture based on LSTM demonstrates a clear advantage over state-of-the-art methods in predicting human decision-making trajectories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed this as a proposal/contribution, while the publication presents demonstrated advantage for the LSTM architecture; this strengthens and revises the original contribution into a result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate a clear advantage over the state-of-the-art methods in predicting human decision making trajectories in both single-agent and multi-agent scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed recurrent neural network architecture based on LSTM demonstrates a clear advantage over state-of-the-art methods in predicting human decision-making trajectories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance advantage claim for the LSTM architecture with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The weights of the top performers in the LSTM networks tend to have a wider distribution and a bigger bias, suggesting possible interpretations for the distribution of strategies adopted by each group. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The weights of the LSTM networks modeling the top performers have a wider distribution and larger bias compared to poor performers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same observed weight-distribution differences with comparable wording and certainty; the claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first application of LSTM methods in the research domain of human decision making. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research represents the first application of LSTM methods in predicting human decision-making in gaming activity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication assert novelty as the first application of LSTMs in this domain; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study successfully reproduced the known photoresponsive behaviour of Ciona intestinalis larvae. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly report reproducing photoresponsive behaviour; this specific reproduction claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ciona intestinalis larvae exhibit sensory arousal and thigmotaxis, which can be modulated by the anxiotropic drug modafinil. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The anxiotropic drug modafinil modulates thigmotactic behaviour. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication separates and explicitly states the modulation by modafinil; it clarifies the modulation component of the preprint claim (the baseline behaviours are also reported elsewhere in the publication).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Crowd size influences the behaviour of Ciona intestinalis larvae. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim mentions crowd-size effects on larval behaviour, so this specific result appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ciona intestinalis larval behaviour can be broken down into a set of stereotyped behaviours that are used to different extents in a context-dependent manner. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: C. intestinalis larval behaviour can be broken down to a set of stereotyped behaviours that are used to different extents in a context-dependent manner. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core conclusion verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study paves the way for comparative quantitative behavioural studies to reconstruct the evolution of behaviour in the chordate lineage. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not explicitly state this forward-looking evolutionary-comparative contribution, so the specific phrasing does not appear in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transcriptomic analysis reveals that over 65% of genes show similar expression patterns between glioblastoma and osteosarcoma. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transcriptomic analysis reveals that over 65% of genes show similar expression patterns between glioblastoma and osteosarcoma. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this transcriptomic similarity result exactly, with identical certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A classification model using eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) achieves high accuracy in distinguishing between glioblastoma and osteosarcoma. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A classification model using eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) achieves high accuracy in distinguishing between glioblastoma and osteosarcoma. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same model-performance claim with equal certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gene expression levels of many SHAP target genes can recapitulate survival differences between glioblastoma and osteosarcoma patients based solely on clinical data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gene expression levels of many SHAP targets can recapitulate survival differences between glioblastoma and osteosarcoma patients based solely on clinical data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same association claim but expresses it with lower certainty (high->medium) and frames it as a conclusion rather than a result, tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: C2ORF72 is identified as a common target from both SHAP target gene lists, representing a promising novel target for glioblastoma and osteosarcoma. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: C2ORF72 is identified as a common target from both lists of SHAP targets, representing a promising potential for diagnosis, prognosis, and therapy of glioblastoma and osteosarcoma. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the identification of C2ORF72 but expands the suggested applications (diagnosis, prognosis, therapy), clarifying and broadening the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved state-of-the-art accuracy on the SKEMPI dataset by incorporating a mutation stability prediction task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved state-of-the-art accuracy on the SKEMPI dataset by incorporating a mutation stability prediction task.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about state-of-the-art performance and the mutation stability task, so meaning and scope are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pythia-PPI increased the Pearson correlation between predictions and experimental data from 0.6447 to 0.7850 on the SKEMPI dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pythia-PPI increased the Pearson's correlation between predictions and experimental data from 0.6447 to 0.7850 on the SKEMPI dataset.
alignments.rationale: Numeric values and dataset match exactly; wording difference is negligible (apostrophe), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pythia-PPI increased the Pearson correlation between predictions and experimental data from 0.3654 to 0.6051 on the virial-receptor dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pythia-PPI increased the Pearson's correlation between predictions and experimental data from 0.3654 to 0.6050 on the viral-receptor dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication corrects the dataset name (viral) and rounds the final digit (0.6050 vs 0.6051); core result unchanged but small clarification/cleanup applied.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings demonstrated Pythia-PPI to be a valuable tool for analyzing the fitness landscape of protein-protein interactions.
alignments.rationale: No equivalent high-level conclusion about analyzing fitness landscapes appears in the publication; instead the paper adds specific experimental validation, so the general conclusion is not present.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental validation confirmed Pythia-PPI's ability to identify high-affinity mutations on the CB6 antibody, with the best single-point mutant showing a 2-fold increase in binding affinity.
alignments.rationale: This experimental validation claim is new in the publication and has no counterpart in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 0.2% of over 300 million protein sequences have experimentally determined functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only 0.2% of over 300 million protein sequences have experimentally determined functions.
alignments.rationale: Identical statistic and wording in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed method predicts whether two proteins catalyze the same enzymatic reaction by analyzing sequence and structural similarities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed method predicts whether two proteins catalyze the same enzymatic reaction by analyzing sequence and structural similarities.
alignments.rationale: Same contribution claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LightGBM-based model outperformed existing approaches in predicting protein functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LightGBM-based model outperformed existing models for predicting protein functions.
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly ('approaches' vs 'models') but the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating sequence and structural information improves the accuracy of protein function prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating sequence and structural information improves the accuracy of protein function prediction.
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion presented in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoMolDesigner is a computational modeling software specifically developed for small-molecule antibiotic design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoMolDesigner is a computational modeling software dedicated to small-molecule antibiotic design.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software includes generative deep learning-enabled molecular generation and automated machine learning based-antibacterial activity/property prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoMolDesigner comprises two functional modules: generative-deep-learning-enabled molecular generation and automated machine-learning-based antibacterial activity/property prediction.
alignments.rationale: Functional modules are described equivalently in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoMolDesigner is open-source, allowing for the incorporation of new features for flexible use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The software is open-source, allowing for the incorporation of new features for flexible use.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication presents this claim with higher certainty (publication certainty labeled high vs preprint medium), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application features a Qt-based graphical user interface that can be run on personal computers with multiple operating systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoMolDesigner can be run on personal computers with multiple operating systems, making it easier to use for experimental scientists.
alignments.rationale: Publication emphasizes usability for experimental scientists and omits the explicit 'Qt-based' detail; wording shifts emphasis and the publication's stated certainty is lower, so the claim is clarified with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Greifswald Sleep Stage Classifier (GSSC) is free, open source, and can be easily installed and used on any moderately powered computer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GSSC is free, open source, and can be relatively easily installed and used on any moderately powered computer.
alignments.rationale: Same contribution claim with only minor wording differences about ease of installation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GSSC has been trained to perform well on a large variety of electrode set-ups, allowing high performance sleep staging with portable systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Greifswald Sleep Stage Classifier (GSSC) has been trained to perform well on a large variety of electrode set-ups.
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the training/electrode-setup claim; the preprint added portable-systems context but the core claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GSSC achieves a level of accuracy equal to, or exceeding, recent state of the art classifiers and human experts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GSSC achieves a level of accuracy equal to, or exceeding, recent state of the art classifiers and human experts.
alignments.rationale: Result claim is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GSSC can be readily integrated into brain-computer interfaces for real-time inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GSSC can be readily integrated into brain-computer interfaces for real-time inference.
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion appears in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dedicated machine learning optimization of reconstruction models is key for achieving the best reconstruction performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dedicated machine learning optimization of reconstruction models is key for achieving the best reconstruction performance.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about the importance of model optimization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual word decoding in reconstructed speech achieves 92-100% accuracy, with a chance level of 8%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual word decoding in reconstructed speech achieves 92%-100% accuracy, with a chance level of 8%.
alignments.rationale: Numeric result and framing are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Direct reconstruction from sensorimotor brain activity produces intelligible speech.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Direct reconstruction from sensorimotor brain activity produces intelligible speech.
alignments.rationale: Core result unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model optimization is necessary for achieving the best speech decoding results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results underline the need for model optimization in achieving best speech decoding results.
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same conclusion with slightly different phrasing; meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reconstruction-based speech decoding from sensorimotor cortex has potential for the development of next-generation BCI technology for communication.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reconstruction-based speech decoding from sensorimotor cortex has potential for the development of next-generation BCI technology for communication.
alignments.rationale: The contribution/implication claim appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SegPath is the largest annotation dataset for the segmentation of haematoxylin and eosin (H&E)-stained sections, being over one order of magnitude larger than publicly available annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SegPath is the largest annotation dataset for the segmentation of hematoxylin and eosin (H&E)-stained sections for eight major cell types in cancer tissue.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the 'largest dataset' claim but adds a specific scope (eight major cell types in cancer tissue), which narrows and clarifies the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SegPath is comparable to, or significantly outperforms, conventional pathologist annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SegPath is comparable with, or outperforms, pathologist annotations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Annotations by pathologists are biased toward typical morphologies, but the model trained on SegPath can overcome this limitation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model trained on SegPath can overcome the bias present in pathologist annotations.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the two-part preprint claim into separate statements; the part about the model overcoming bias is preserved and presented more explicitly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed pipeline provides foundational datasets for the histopathology machine learning community.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide foundational datasets for machine-learning research in histopathology.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution claim (foundational datasets for histopathology ML) with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Strainflow captured the rise in COVID-19 cases two months ahead of the Delta and Omicron surges in most countries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strainflow was trained and validated on 0.9 million sequences and was able to capture the rise in COVID-19 cases 2 months ahead of the Delta and Omicron surges.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the two-month-ahead prediction claim but omits the phrase 'in most countries', so the claim is slightly narrowed/revised in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Strainflow was trained and validated on 0.9 million SARS-CoV-2 genome sequences from December 2019 to June 2021.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strainflow was trained and validated on 0.9 million sequences and was able to capture the rise in COVID-19 cases 2 months ahead of the Delta and Omicron surges.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly restates the training/validation on 0.9M sequences; the content and certainty are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Entropy analysis of Strainflow unsupervised embeddings reveals explore-exploit cycles in genomic feature-space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Entropy analysis of Strainflow unsupervised embeddings reveals explore-exploit cycles in genomic feature-space, enhancing the interpretability of the model.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same entropy/explore–exploit finding and adds that this enhances model interpretability, a clarification rather than a change in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Strainflow is openly available as an interactive web-application for prospective genomic surveillance of COVID-19.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strainflow is available as an interactive web-application for prospective genomic surveillance of COVID-19 globally.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves availability as a web app but explicitly states global prospective surveillance, which broadens the scope of the availability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a method to identify the optimal colour that either minimises or maximises visibility in natural environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a method to identify the optimal colour that either minimizes or maximizes visibility in natural environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the proposed methodological contribution verbatim; no substantive change detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach can predict robustly the most and least effective colours for camouflage using a comparatively small number of samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comparatively small number of samples can be used to predict robustly the most and least effective colours for camouflage in temperate forest and semi-arid desert environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the predictive capacity with small sample sizes and explicitly names the tested environments; content and certainty are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Colour strongly affected detection time for simulated dichromatic observers in both temperate forest and semi-arid desert environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For simulated dichromatic observers, colour strongly affected detection time for both environments.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result about dichromatic observers and detection time is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Normal trichromatic observers were far more effective at breaking camouflage and detecting targets compared to simulated dichromatic observers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Trichromatic observers were more effective at breaking camouflage compared to dichromatic observers.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same directional result but softens language ('far more' -> 'more') and downgrades certainty to medium, so the claim is clarified and slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning improves accuracy in nucleus segmentation compared to classical image processing algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning improves accuracy in nucleus segmentation compared to classical image processing algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance improvement claim verbatim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed evaluation framework measures accuracy, types of errors, and computational efficiency in nucleus segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed evaluation framework measures accuracy, types of errors, and computational efficiency in nucleus segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution (evaluation framework) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning can reduce the number of biologically relevant errors in nucleus segmentation by half.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning can reduce the number of biologically relevant errors in nucleus segmentation by half.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative error-reduction result is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A set of 23,165 manually annotated nuclei and source code for reproducing experiments is publicly released.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A set of 23,165 manually annotated nuclei and source code for reproducing experiments has been publicly released.
alignments.rationale: The data and code release claim is restated with equivalent content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The msDyNODE model successfully captures multiscale brain activity using both simulations and electrophysiological experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The msDyNODE model successfully captures multiscale activity using both simulations and electrophysiological experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core result about capturing multiscale activity across simulation and electrophysiology with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The causal interactions derived from msDyNODE align well with the hierarchical neuroanatomy of the mammalian central nervous system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The causal interactions derived from msDyNODE align well with the hierarchical neuroanatomy of the mammalian central nervous system.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same alignment claim but with higher stated certainty (publication labels it high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The msDyNODE model exhibits behavioral dependences in its derived causal interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The causal interactions exhibited behavioral dependences.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the behavioral dependence finding with similar wording and the same medium certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method eliminates the need for downsampling faster sampling signals, thus preserving dynamic information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work offers a new approach for mechanistic multiscale studies of neural processes.
alignments.rationale: The specific technical claim about eliminating downsampling is not explicitly stated in the publication, which instead frames the contribution more generally as a new approach for multiscale studies, thus weakening/summarizing the original technical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-scale modeling framework is adaptable and can integrate various well-established models depending on the neural recording modalities used.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work offers a new approach for mechanistic multiscale studies of neural processes.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a general contribution statement but does not explicitly mention adaptability or model integration; the specific adaptability claim is therefore presented in a more general, less explicit form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DANSE model efficiently estimates brain maps and preserves all features of the NLLS approach with significant improvements including noise-suppression and computation speed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DANSE method efficiently estimates R2t* and R2' maps with significant improvements in noise suppression and computation speed.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core performance improvements for DANSE focused on R2t*/R2' maps; wording is slightly streamlined, so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DANSE-generated R 2 t * and R 2′ maps had three times smaller errors than that of the NLLS method for data with typical GRE SNR characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DANSE-generated R2t* and R2' maps had up to three times smaller errors than that of the NLLS method in low signal-to-noise ratio data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the quantitative improvement but specifies it applies in low-SNR data (a narrower characterization than 'typical GRE SNR'), so the claim is clarified and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DANSE method enables fast reconstruction of magnetic-field-inhomogeneity-free and noise-suppressed quantitative qGRE brain maps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DANSE method enables fast reconstruction of qGRE maps with significantly reduced sensitivity to noise and magnetic field inhomogeneities.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the absolute wording ('inhomogeneity-free') to 'reduced sensitivity', so the substance is retained but the claim is revised and its confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DANSE does not require any information about field inhomogeneities during application.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DANSE method does not require any information about field inhomogeneities during application.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical claim verbatim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The features of DANSE, along with fast computational speed, can lead to broad qGRE clinical and research applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The features of the DANSE method can lead to broad qGRE clinical and research applications.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same contribution-level claim about potential broad application; wording is effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DEMINERS integrates an RNA multiplexing experimental workflow, a machine-learning barcode classifier based on Random Forest, and a novel basecaller built on an optimized convolutional neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DEMINERS offers a robust, high-throughput solution for precise transcript and RNA modification analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the method's high-level capability rather than listing components; mapping is plausible but indirect since the architecture description in the preprint is not spelled out in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DEMINERS can demultiplex up to 24 samples while substantially reducing the required RNA input and running time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DEMINERS enables accurate demultiplexing of up to 24 samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the demultiplexing capacity (24 samples) but splits other performance aspects into a separate claim (runtime/RNA input); this alignment captures the core demultiplexing component.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DEMINERS revealed altered airway microbial diversity in COVID-19 and a potential role of m 6 A in increasing transcriptomic diversity in glioma and the mature blood-stage of malaria parasites.
alignments.rationale: These specific biological findings are not represented in the publication claims provided, so the claim appears to have been removed or not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DEMINERS is a simple, robust, high-throughput DRS method for accurately estimating transcript levels, poly(A) lengths, and mutation and RNA modification heterogeneity at single-read level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DEMINERS offers a robust, high-throughput solution for precise transcript and RNA modification analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's conclusion about DEMINERS as a robust high-throughput solution for transcript and RNA modification analysis, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GFETM demonstrates superior accuracy and generalizability in scATAC-seq data analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GFETM demonstrates superior accuracy and generalizability in scATAC-seq analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core performance claim directly; wording is effectively identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GFETM captures cell-state specific TF activity through zero-shot inference and attention mechanism analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GFETM captures cell-state-specific transcription factor activity with both zero-shot inference and attention-mechanism analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this technical result with matched wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The topic mixtures inferred by GFETM reveal biologically meaningful epigenomic signatures of kidney diabetes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The topic mixtures inferred by GFETM reveal biologically meaningful epigenomic signatures of kidney diabetes.
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved but the publication lists a lower certainty (medium) than the preprint (high), so confidence is tempered while the semantic content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DCNNs trained in object recognition acquire representations that closely capture human conceptual judgements about the co-occurrence of animals and their typical scenes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DCNNs trained in object recognition acquire representations that closely capture human conceptual judgements about the co-occurrence of animals and their typical scenes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this principal finding verbatim; no substantive change detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representational hierarchy of DCNNs shows surprising similarities with the representational transformations emerging in domain-specific ventrotemporal areas up to domain-general frontoparietal areas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representational hierarchy of DCNNs shows surprising similarities with the representational transformations emerging in domain-specific ventrotemporal areas up to domain-general frontoparietal areas.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this claim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability of neural networks to learn a human-like high-level conceptual representation of object-scene co-occurrence depends upon the amount of object-scene co-occurrence present in the image set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability of neural networks to learn a human-like high-level conceptual representation of object-scene co-occurrence depends upon the amount of object-scene co-occurrence present in the image set.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mid/high-level DCNN layers represent the category division for animals and scenes as observed in VTC, but their information content shows reduced domain-specific representational richness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mid/high-level DCNN layers represent the category division for animals and scenes as observed in VTC, but their information content shows reduced domain-specific representational richness.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical observation without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The information processing strategies employed by human and artificial visual systems reveal unknown similarities and differences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Testing within- and between-domain selectivity while manipulating contextual regularities reveals unknown similarities and differences in the information processing strategies employed by human and artificial visual systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same high-level conclusion but ties it to a specific testing approach and indicates lower certainty (publication lists low certainty), so the claim is clarified and its confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study identified distinct platelet subpopulations that are associated with disease severity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct platelet subpopulations correlate with disease severity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core result that platelet subpopulations correlate with severity; wording is effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrated that alterations in platelet transcription patterns can exacerbate endotheliopathy, potentially heightening the risk of coagulation in fatal patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Changes in platelet transcription patterns can intensify endotheliopathy, increasing the risk of coagulation in fatal cases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhanced transcriptional heterogeneity in activated platelets is linked to adverse outcomes in conditions such as sepsis, COVID-19, and autoimmune diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These changes may impact lymphocyte function, indicating a more extensive role for platelets in inflammatory and immune responses.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from a direct linkage to adverse outcomes in specific diseases to a broader potential role affecting lymphocyte function and immune responses; this represents a revision and broadening with somewhat lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The discovery of these unique platelet subpopulations paves the way for innovative therapeutic strategies targeting platelet activation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings pave the way for innovative therapeutic approaches targeting platelet activation, which could improve patient outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution claim but explicitly links potential therapies to improved patient outcomes, clarifying the expected impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biologically realistic neural architectures perform optimally when they display critical dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biologically realistic neural architectures perform best when they display critical dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance is driven by network topology, and the modular organization of large-scale functional systems is computationally relevant. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Performance is driven by network topology and the modular organization of intrinsic networks is computationally relevant. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about topology and modular organization with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a prominent interaction between network structure and dynamics, allowing the same underlying architecture to support a wide range of learning capacities across dynamical regimes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The same underlying architecture can support a wide range of memory capacity values as well as different functions, depending on the dynamical regime the network is in. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work opens new opportunities to discover how the network organization of the brain optimizes cognitive capacity. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work opens new opportunities to discover how the network organization of the brain optimizes cognitive capacity. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is carried over verbatim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method can generalize a single model across Hi-C resolutions, multiple restriction enzymes, and multiple cell populations while maintaining reconstruction accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method can accurately generalize a single model across Hi-C resolutions, multiple restriction enzymes, and multiple cell populations while maintaining reconstruction accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific generalization claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our algorithm outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in the accuracy of prediction and introduces a novel method for 3D structure prediction from Hi-C data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiC-GNN outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in accuracy of prediction and runtime. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiC-GNN is unique from other methods for chromosome structure prediction in that it learns in an eager setting rather than a lazy setting. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This generalization capability is not present in any existing methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models learned by HiC-GNN can be generalized to data that is distinct from the training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's absolute exclusivity claim about other methods is removed; the publication states their model generalizes without asserting others cannot, so the original absolute claim is tempered and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method is more robust to contact variance than the compared methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A data set of 20-30 images is sufficient to accurately train the classifier using our machine learning approach based on topological data analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TDAExplore can classify different types of cellular perturbations after training with only 20-30 high-resolution images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method quantifies differences between groups of images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach identifies subcellular regions with the largest dissimilarities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TDAExplore provides quantitative, spatial information, characterizing which image regions contribute to classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication directly expresses the same ability to localize image regions driving classification, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: When data leakage is avoided by minimizing sequence similarities between training and test sets, performances become random. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicting protein-protein interactions remains an unsolved task for proteins showing little sequence similarity to previously studied proteins. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel objective function used in the analysis provides a conveniently rugged landscape for assessing the effectiveness of differential evolution hyperparameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Adapter removal is unnecessary when calling germline variants, but preprocessing methods should be carefully chosen when developing and using machine learning-based variant analysis methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: singleDeep provides valuable insights into cell types and gene importance estimation for phenotypic characterization. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some interferon signature genes are consistently relevant for autoimmunity across all immune cell types in lupus. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some interferon signature genes are consistently relevant for autoimmunity across all immune cell types in lupus. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific lupus interferon-gene finding is carried forward verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genes linked to dementia, such as APOE, have relevant roles in specific brain cell populations like astrocytes. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of scRNA-Seq data requires new algorithms due to the unique characteristics of the data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The minimum mean error distance between the motor hotspot locations identified by TMS and our proposed motor-hotspot-identification approach was 0.22 ± 0.03 cm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The minimum mean error distance between the motor hotspot locations identified by TMS and our proposed motor-hotspot-identification approach was 0.22 ± 0.03 cm. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A mean error distance of 1.32 ± 0.15 cm was measured when using only nine channels attached to the middle of the motor cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A mean error distance of 1.32 ± 0.15 cm was measured when using only nine channels attached to the middle of the motor cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward this specific measured error distance unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrated the feasibility of our novel EEG-based motor-hotspot-identification method. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrated the feasibility of our novel EEG-based motor-hotspot-identification method. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The feasibility conclusion is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach can be used as an alternative to TMS for motor hotspot identification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach can be used as an alternative to TMS for motor hotspot identification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim that the method can serve as a TMS alternative.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usability of our approach would significantly increase when using a recently developed portable tES device integrated with an EEG device. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not include this specific usability/portable tES integration claim, so it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A portable, universal, DNA-based tool can greatly facilitate in-situ monitoring of elasmobranch trade. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A portable, universal, DNA-based tool can greatly facilitate in-situ monitoring of endangered elasmobranch species. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm successfully distinguished 25 out of 28 elasmobranch species, including 20 CITES-listed species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study successfully distinguished 25 out of 28 shark and ray species using a deep learning algorithm based on DNA melt-curve signatures. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved 79.41% accuracy for classifying 28 elasmobranch species using qPCR fluorescence spectra. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's top-level claims do not state the specific 79.41% accuracy figure or the qPCR fluorescence spectra metric, so the numeric accuracy claim is not retained at the claim level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further optimization of the barcode regions will significantly enhance the tool's effectiveness for monitoring elasmobranch DNA sequence variation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include an explicit forward-looking claim about barcode-region optimization, so this specific recommendation appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Twenty of the distinguished species are CITES-listed, indicating the method's relevance for monitoring trade in endangered species. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes an explicit conservation/trade relevance claim (20 CITES-listed species); while related material appears in the preprint, this specific conclusion is presented separately in the publication's claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can improve monitoring of the elasmobranch trade worldwide without the need for a lab or species-specific assays. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an operational claim about global, lab-free monitoring applicability that is not stated as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Anomaly detection methods can automatically identify unexpected properties of proteins. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Anomaly detection methods can automatically identify unexpected properties of proteins. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the general conclusion that anomaly detection can find unexpected protein properties.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method generates meaningful representations without labeled inputs using pretrained deep neural network models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method generates meaningful representations without labeled inputs using pretrained deep neural network models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution about unsupervised representation learning with pretrained models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The anomaly score is useful in 3D folding-related segmentation of proteins. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The anomaly score is useful in 3D folding-related segmentation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the anomaly score's utility in 3D folding-related segmentation tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of protein language models and anomaly detection techniques is effective for discovering a range of protein characteristics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of pLM and anomaly detection techniques is a valid method for discovering a range of global and local protein characteristics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the combined approach as valid for discovering diverse protein characteristics, matching the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel method shows improved performance over strong baselines across various tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The protein language models (pLM) can detect anomalies in function, phylogenetic families, and segmentation tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes detection capabilities across tasks but does not explicitly claim improved performance over strong baselines, so the performance-over-baselines claim is softened/reframed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning protein structure prediction models like RosettaFold and AlphaFold2 have significantly impacted the structural biology field. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning-based protein structure prediction algorithms have significantly impacted the structural biology field and drug discovery. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Preliminary studies have addressed the usage of these models in virtual screening, but none have focused on hit-finding in real-world scenarios with low sequence identity targets. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication's headline claims do not restate this specific limitation about low-sequence-identity hit-finding as a distinct claim, so it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using out-of-the-box AlphaFold2 models is not an ideal scenario for rigid receptor ligand docking. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using out-of-the-box AlphaFold2 models is not ideal for virtual screening campaigns. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the docking-specific critique into a broader statement about virtual screening, widening the scope of the limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inclusion of post-processing modeling may be necessary to create a more realistic holo target model for binding site predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Post-processing modeling is recommended to create a more realistic holo model for binding site analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the recommendation for post-processing to produce realistic holo models and presents it with stronger emphasis/certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to obtain quantitatively accurate results using AlphaFold2 models in conjunction with state-of-the-art free energy perturbation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a new, specific result about combining AlphaFold2 with free energy perturbation that was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepDetect, a BiLSTM-based algorithm, improves the accuracy of peptide detectability prediction compared to existing algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepDetect, a BiLSTM-based algorithm, improves prediction accuracy for peptide detectability enhanced by peptide digestibility.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim that DeepDetect improves detectability prediction but adds the role of peptide digestibility as an enhancing factor, clarifying the mechanism rather than changing the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepDetect demonstrates wide applicability to commonly used proteases including Trypsin, ArgC, Chymotrypsin, GluC, LysC, AspN, LysN, and LysargiNase.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim addresses applicability across specific proteases; the protease-specific applicability statement from the preprint is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On eleven independent test data sets, DeepDetect achieved AUCs of 0.858~0.957, outperforming state-of-the-art algorithms AP3 and PepFormer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepDetect achieved higher prediction accuracies than PepFormer on 11 test data sets from E. coli, yeast, mouse, and human samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of outperforming PepFormer on 11 datasets but omits the specific AUC range and the comparison to AP3, narrowing and revising the comparative performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted peptide detectability from DeepDetect can be utilized to re-rank and validate peptide-spectrum matches, potentially enhancing the sensitivity of peptide identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepDetect can significantly accelerate the library search in data-independent acquisition mass spectrometry data analysis without loss of peptide and protein identification sensitivity.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a different downstream application (accelerating library search in DIA) and reframes the sensitivity effect as no loss rather than potential enhancement, so the intended application and implication for sensitivity are revised and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ResMiCo is substantially more accurate than the state of the art in reference-free misassembly prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ResMiCo is substantially more accurate than the state of the art in reference-free misassembly prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's core performance claim verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ResMiCo estimated 4.7% misassembled contigs per metagenome across multiple real-world datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ResMiCo estimated 7% misassembled contigs per metagenome across multiple real-world datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy, robustness, and ease-of-use of ResMiCo make the tool suitable for general quality control of metagenome assemblies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy, robustness, and ease-of-use of ResMiCo make the tool suitable for general quality control of metagenome assemblies.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conclusion about suitability for QC with the same emphasis on accuracy, robustness, and ease-of-use, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing models for misassembly prediction lack the accuracy needed for robust quality control of metagenome assemblies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ResMiCo can be used to optimize metagenome assembly hyperparameters to improve accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report using ResMiCo to optimize assembly hyperparameters; this proposed application from the preprint is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel deep learning approach for miRNA target prediction outperforms existing prediction methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel deep learning approach for miRNA target prediction outperforms existing prediction methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The entire miRNA, rather than just the seed region, plays a significant role in functional targeting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Site accessibility is a factor in targeting but cannot be used as the sole indicator for functionality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study collected and curated more than 150,000 experimentally validated miRNA:gene targets to train the deep learning network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study collected over 150,000 experimentally validated homo sapiens miRNA:gene targets to enhance target prediction accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reliability scores are a new concept for scientific machine learning studies that assess the ability of a classifier to produce a reliable classification for a given instance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SWIF(r) reliability score (SRS) reflects the trustworthiness of the classification of a specific instance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SWIF(r) Reliability Score (SRS) demonstrates utility when faced with common challenges in machine learning, such as unknown classes in testing data and systemic mismatches between training and testing data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SRS demonstrates utility in addressing common challenges in machine learning, such as unknown classes in testing data and systemic mismatches between training and testing data.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The SRS allows researchers to thoroughly interrogate their data and training approach while integrating domain-specific knowledge with machine learning frameworks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: trRosettaX-Single outperforms AlphaFold2 and RoseTTAFold on natural proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: trRosettaX-Single outperforms AlphaFold2 and RoseTTAFold on orphan proteins and works well on human-designed proteins.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: trRosettaX-Single works very well on 101 human-designed proteins, with an accuracy (average TM-score 0.77) approaching AlphaFold2 and higher than RoseTTAFold, but using much less computing resource.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The full trRosettaX-Single pipeline is two times faster than AlphaFold2, using much fewer computing resources.

alignments.pub_claim_text: trRosettaX-Single generates structure models with high confidence on 2,000 designed proteins from network hallucination.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopaminergic plasticity at corticostriatal synapses alters competition between striatal pathways, impacting the evidence accumulation process during decision-making. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopaminergic plasticity at corticostriatal synapses alters competition between striatal pathways, impacting the evidence accumulation process during decision-making. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim with equivalent certainty and role as a result, indicating no change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rate of evidence accumulation varies with inter-channel differences in direct pathway activity while boundary height varies with overall indirect pathway activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rate of evidence accumulation varied with inter-channel differences in direct pathway activity while boundary height varied with overall indirect pathway activity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mechanistic relationship but with slightly reduced stated certainty (high→medium), so the claim is preserved but tempered in confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This multi-level modeling approach demonstrates how complementary learning and decision computations can emerge from corticostriatal plasticity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This multi-level modeling approach demonstrates how complementary learning and decision computations can emerge from corticostriatal plasticity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears unchanged in content and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopaminergic feedback alters the network-level dynamics of cortico-basal-ganglia-thalamic pathways during learning to bias behavioral choice towards more rewarding actions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spike-timing dependent plasticity simulations showed that dopaminergic feedback based on rewards modified the ratio of direct and indirect corticostriatal weights within opposing action channels. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning has significantly advanced the development of high-performance methods for protein function prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning has significantly advanced the development of high-performance methods for protein function prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Template information remains an indispensable component in most cases for function prediction methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Large-scale benchmarking and blind testing in the 5th Critical Assessment of Function Annotation (CAFA5) demonstrate StarFunc's advantage over both state-of-the-art deep learning methods and conventional template-based predictors. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: StarFunc integrates state-of-the-art deep learning models with template information from various sources. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CPDiffusion effectively captures highly conserved residues and sequence features for a specific protein family without relying on extensive training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CPDiffusion effectively captures highly conserved residues and sequence features for specific protein families without relying on extensive training data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of the generated proteins for both KmAgo and PfAgo show unambiguous activity in DNA cleavage, with many exhibiting superior activity compared to the wild-type. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CPDiffusion facilitates the design of enzymes with multi-domain molecular structures and intricate functions through in silico generation and screening. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CPDiffusion demonstrates a remarkable success rate in generating novel sequences for proteins with complex structures and functions in a single step. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning of a suitable cognitive map of the problem space suffices for planning and problem solving. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning a suitable cognitive map of the problem space suffices for planning and problem solving. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting cognitive map encodes relations between actions and observations, providing a quasi-Euclidean sense of direction for reaching distant goals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cognitive map encodes relations between actions and observations, providing a quasi-Euclidean sense of direction for reaching distant goals. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This method speeds up learning of navigation in 2D mazes and locomotion with complex actuator systems. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method speeds up learning of navigation in 2D mazes and locomotion with complex actuator systems. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed cognitive map learner does not require a teacher and does not need backpropagation of errors or large datasets for learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed cognitive map learner does not require a teacher and does not need backpropagation of errors or large datasets for learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cognitive map learner provides a blueprint for future energy-efficient neuromorphic hardware that acquires advanced cognitive capabilities through autonomous on-chip learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cognitive map learner provides a blueprint for future energy-efficient neuromorphic hardware that acquires advanced cognitive capabilities through autonomous on-chip learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The forward-looking contribution about neuromorphic hardware remains the same in content and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proof-of-concept study demonstrates that significant disease severity mitigation can potentially be accomplished with existing anti-mediator drugs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant disease severity mitigation can potentially be accomplished with existing anti-mediator drugs through a multi-modal, adaptive treatment policy implemented with AI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DRL trained an AI policy that could improve outcomes from a baseline mortality rate of 41% to a recovery rate of 82.3% over 42 days simulated time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates that a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) policy can improve the Recovered Rate from 61% to 90% in simulated sepsis scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of simulation-based, model-free deep reinforcement learning is necessary to develop effective treatment regimens for controlling cytokine storms. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current goal of this work is to inspire the development of a research ecosystem that improves simulation models and develops sensing/assay technologies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Exogenous testosterone fully eliminated strategic, i.e., feigned, prosociality and thus decreased submission to audience expectations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exogenous testosterone fully eliminated strategic, i.e., feigned, prosociality and thus decreased submission to audience expectations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Testosterone compared to placebo did not deteriorate reinforcement learning per se. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When being watched, testosterone altered the degree to which the learned information on choice value translated to action selection. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When being watched, testosterone altered the degree to which the learned information on choice value translated to action selection. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides novel evidence of testosterone’s effects on implicit reward processing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OligoRL demonstrates how reinforcement learning offers a general solution for complex oligo design problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OligoRL demonstrates how RL offers a general solution for complex oligo design problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OligoRL allows 'black-box' design criteria and can be adapted to solve many oligo design problems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OligoRL allows 'black-box' design criteria and can be adapted to solve many oligo design problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OligoRL has been used to solve three distinct design problems related to oligo design. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OligoRL was used to find pools of random DNA barcodes that lack restriction enzyme recognition sequences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
