alignments.preprint_claim_text: The text-based simulation paradigm is well-suited for modeling complex living systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SimulateGPT increased correlation coefficients and precision in predicting progression-free survival in cancer patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication offers concrete quantitative support in a particular biomedical prediction (progression-free survival), narrowing the original general claim while providing stronger evidence for that application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs enable a new class of versatile and broadly applicable biological simulators. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SimulateGPT's predictions were rated favorably by biomedical scientists compared to direct GPT-4 inference in qualitative experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a specific qualitative evaluation rather than the broad claim of versatile, broadly applicable simulators; the claim is therefore narrowed and presented more cautiously in implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The behavior and dynamics of dopamine signaling underlying sodium appetite can be accounted for by extending principles of homeostatic regulation into a reinforcement learning framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The behavior and dynamics of dopamine signaling underlying sodium appetite can be accounted for by a homeostatically regulated reinforcement learning framework (HRRL). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the main modeling result using equivalent terminology (HRRL), preserving scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HRRL-based agents successfully reproduced the state and taste dependence of behavioral responding for sodium as well as for lithium and potassium salts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HRRL-based agents successfully reproduced the state and taste dependence of behavioral responding for sodium as well as for lithium and potassium salts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The agent-based reproduction result is carried over exactly from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine signals evoked by sodium decrease with satiety and increase with deprivation, suggesting that phasic dopamine signals and sodium consumption are down regulated prior to animals reaching satiety. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopamine signals evoked by sodium decrease with satiety and increase with deprivation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same empirical observation but omits the additional interpretive suggestion and reports it with higher certainty, thus clarifying and strengthening the core observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mice cannot distinguish between sodium and lithium containing salts, as evidenced by HRRL agents exhibiting an equal preference for both solutions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the explicit behavioral claim about mice being unable to distinguish sodium vs lithium; instead it focuses on agent reproduction of state/taste dependence, so this specific animal-level claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Appetite-driven behavior may be driven by reinforcement learning mechanisms that are dynamically tuned by homeostatic need. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Appetite-driven behavior may be driven by reinforcement learning mechanisms that are dynamically tuned by homeostatic need. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretive conclusion about reinforcement-learning–tuned homeostasis is preserved unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most tested audio neural network models out-predicted previous filter-bank models of auditory cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most tested audio neural network models outpredicted standard spectromporal filter-bank models of auditory cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result comparing neural network models to filter-bank baselines is replicated verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Middle stages of the models best predicted primary auditory cortex while deep stages best predicted non-primary cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Middle stages of the models best predicted primary auditory cortex, while deep stages best predicted non-primary cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The layer-to-area mapping claim is unchanged in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained to recognize speech in background noise produced better brain predictions than models trained to recognize speech in quiet. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained to recognize speech in background noise produced better brain predictions than models trained to recognize speech in quiet. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This training-condition result is present with the same qualification and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training task influenced the prediction quality for specific cortical tuning properties, with best overall predictions resulting from models trained on multiple tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training task influenced the prediction quality for specific cortical tuning properties, with best overall predictions resulting from models trained on multiple tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same, but the publication reports a lower certainty level, so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current models do not explain auditory cortical responses in their entirety. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current models do not explain auditory cortical responses in their entirety. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding caveat about model limitations remains the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DeepCell Kiosk is a cloud-native software that dynamically scales deep learning workflows for large imaging datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeepCell Kiosk is a cloud-native software that dynamically scales deep learning workflows for large imaging datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software identified cell nuclei in 10 6 1-megapixel images in approximately 5.5 hours for a cost of around $250. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The software identified cell nuclei in 106 1-megapixel images in approximately 5.5 hours for around US$250. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance/cost result is the same, with only formatting differences in the numeric presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A sub-$100 cost is achievable depending on the cluster configuration used with the DeepCell Kiosk. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A cost below US$100 is achievable depending on cluster configuration when using the DeepCell Kiosk. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The cost-conclusion is preserved with equivalent caveats and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning method can design human Immunoglobulin G (IgG) antibodies with target affinities that are superior to candidates from phage display panning experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ens-Grad is a machine learning method that can design complementarity determining regions of human Immunoglobulin G antibodies with target affinities superior to those derived from phage display panning experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication names the method (Ens-Grad) and specifies it acts on CDRs, which clarifies the preprint's more general claim while keeping the core result the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can improve target-specificity by the modular composition of models from different experimental campaigns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can improve target specificity by the modular composition of models from different experimental campaigns.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive and differentiable models of antibody binding can be learned from high-throughput experimental data without the need for target structural data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive and differentiable models of antibody binding can be learned from high-throughput experimental data without the need for target structural data.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same conclusion about learning models from high-throughput data without structural information.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With sufficient target-specific training data, machine learning can suggest novel antibody variable domain sequences that are superior to those observed during training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ens-Grad is a machine learning method that can design complementarity determining regions of human Immunoglobulin G antibodies with target affinities superior to those derived from phage display panning experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes designing CDRs with affinities superior to phage-display candidates rather than explicitly claiming generation of novel variable-domain sequences superior to training examples, shifting the claim's focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data from disparate antibody campaigns can be combined by machine learning to improve antibody specificity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can improve target specificity by the modular composition of models from different experimental campaigns.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same idea of combining models from different campaigns to improve specificity, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mime streamlined the process of developing predictive models with high accuracy, leveraging complex datasets to identify critical genes associated with prognosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed computational framework, Mime, streamlines the process of developing predictive models with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same contribution of Mime streamlining predictive model development with high accuracy; wording is consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An in silico combined model based on de novo PIEZO1-associated signatures constructed by Mime demonstrated high accuracy in predicting outcomes of patients compared with other published models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An in silico combined model based on de novo PIEZO1-associated signatures constructed by Mime demonstrated high accuracy in predicting patient outcomes compared with other published models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about the combined model's high predictive accuracy versus published models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PIEZO1-associated signatures could also precisely infer immunotherapy response by applying different algorithms in Mime.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PIEZO1-associated signatures could precisely infer immunotherapy response by applying different algorithms in Mime.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same claim about inferring immunotherapy response using the PIEZO1-associated signatures and Mime algorithms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SDC1 selected from PIEZO1-associated signatures presented high-potential role in glioma with targeted prospect.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SDC1 selected from the PIEZO1-associated signatures demonstrated high potential as a glioma target.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens and clarifies the preprint's suggestion by wording it as a demonstrated high potential and raising certainty from medium to high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Intra-subject random forest machine learning measures classify upper limb use more accurately than other measures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intra-subject random forest machine learning measure detected upper limb use more accurately than all other measures.
alignments.rationale: Both texts claim the intra-subject random forest outperforms other measures in detecting/classifying upper limb use; wording differences are minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest measure uses information about the orientation and the amount of movement of the forearm to detect upper limb use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intra-subject random forest machine learning measure detected upper limb use more accurately than all other measures.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the measure's superior detection but does not state the specific features (orientation/amount of movement) used, so the mechanistic claim from the preprint is not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The types of movements and the proportion of functional data included in the training/testing set influence the performance of machine learning measures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of machine learning measures was influenced by the types of movements and the proportion of functional data in the training/testing datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about movement types and functional-data proportion affecting performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is currently no knowledge of the information used by machine learning methods for detecting upper limb use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents a step towards understanding and optimizing measures for upper limb use assessment using wearable sensors.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's absolute statement of 'no knowledge' is softened in the publication to framing the work as a step toward understanding, clarifying the limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study presents the first step towards understanding and optimizing machine learning methods for upper limb use assessment using wearable sensors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents a step towards understanding and optimizing measures for upper limb use assessment using wearable sensors.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution as the preprint about being a step toward understanding and optimization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning pipeline estimates flower cover and detects and classifies arthropod taxa from time-lapse recordings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning pipeline estimates flower cover and detects and classifies arthropod taxa from time-lapse recordings.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution about the pipeline's capabilities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The filtering approach has been demonstrated to significantly decrease the incidence of false positives in arthropod detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The filtering approach has been demonstrated to significantly decrease the incidence of false positives in arthropod detection.
alignments.rationale: The claim about the filtering approach reducing false positives is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final selected model, EfficientNetB4, achieved an 80% average precision on randomly selected samples when applied to the complete pipeline.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final selected model, EfficientNetB4, achieved an 80% average precision on randomly selected samples when applied to the complete pipeline.
alignments.rationale: The reported model performance (80% average precision) is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reduced flower cover correlates with a noticeable drop in arthropod detections during the beginning and end of the season.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reduced flower cover correlates with a noticeable drop in arthropod detections during the beginning and end of the season.
alignments.rationale: The ecological correlation between flower cover and arthropod detections is carried over intact into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The emitter’s sex can be successfully predicted from their spectrograms using deep neural networks, achieving approximately 84% correct classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks (DNNs) can classify the sex of emitting mice from their ultrasonic vocalizations (USVs) with unprecedented performance of 77%.
alignments.rationale: Both claim DNN sex classification, but the reported accuracy changed from ~84% in the preprint to 77% in the publication, reducing the numerical performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vocalization characteristics of individual mice only contribute mildly to the classification accuracy, with sex-only classification reaching about 78%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the DNN increases to 85% when individual properties of each mouse are included during training.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a substantial performance increase (to 85%) when individual properties are included, which contradicts the preprint's characterization of only mild contribution and strengthens the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the deep neural networks in classifying sex exceeds that of other techniques such as Support Vector Machines and Ridge regression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks (DNNs) can classify the sex of emitting mice from their ultrasonic vocalizations (USVs) with unprecedented performance of 77%.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes DNN performance but does not explicitly report comparisons to SVM or Ridge models, so the comparative claim from the preprint is not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Male and female vocalizations differ in their spectrotemporal structure, which is recognizable even in single vocalizations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A detailed analysis of USV structure reveals that a subset of male vocalizations is characterized by a few acoustic features, while sex differences rely on a complex combination of many features.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more detailed account of the spectrotemporal differences and their feature basis, clarifying the preprint's general statement about recognizable differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional neural network (CNN) models learned from the TF binding changes in the promoter to predict the splicing pattern changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network (CNN) models, trained on TF binding changes in the promoters, can predict the changes in splicing patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about CNN models trained on promoter TF binding changes predicting splicing; wording and scope are essentially identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several transcription factors (TFs) were identified that considerably reduced the model performance of splicing prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using empirical validation with K562 CTCFL shRNA knock-down data, we showed the significant role of CTCFL in splicing regulation.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted multiple TFs affecting model performance, while the paper presents experimental validation for a single TF (CTCFL); this narrows the scope but strengthens (amplifies) evidence for that specific factor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Promoter-bound transcription factor binding sites (TFBSs) influence the regulation of downstream splicing patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings highlight the potential role of promoter-bound TFBSs in influencing the regulation of downstream splicing patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the claim more cautiously as a "potential role," clarifying and slightly tempering the original conclusion while retaining its scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models demonstrated potential in predicting splicing patterns based on TFBS occupancies.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a broader statement about machine learning models generally that was not explicitly stated in the preprint (which focused on CNNs), so it is treated as an added, more general claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: There are limitations in the generalization of predicting the splicing forms of singleton genes across diverse tissues.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a limitation about generalization across tissues that does not appear in the preprint and thus is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human-to-human variability in the quantification of dendritic spines is substantial, with an inter-rater reliability of 82.2±6.4%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human-to-human variability in quantifying dendritic spines is substantial, with an inter-rater reliability of 82.2±6.4%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same measured inter-rater reliability with identical values and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepD3 is an open deep learning-based framework that quantifies dendritic spines in microscopy data in a fully automated fashion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepD3 is an open deep learning-based framework that quantifies dendritic spines in microscopy data in a fully automated fashion.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is restated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepD3's neural networks provide precise quantification of dendrites and dendritic spines across various datasets and experimental conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepD3's neural networks provide precise quantification of dendrites and dendritic spines across various datasets and conditions.
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved with equivalent wording and scope between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DeepD3 framework addresses the lack of openly available datasets of dendritic spines and offers a reproducible spine quantification method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeepD3 framework addresses the lack of openly available datasets of dendritic spines and offers a reproducible spine quantification method.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about dataset availability and reproducibility as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoGater is a weakly supervised deep learning model that can separate healthy populations from unhealthy and dead populations using only light-scatter channels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoGater is a weakly supervised deep learning model that can separate healthy populations from unhealthy and dead populations using only light-scatter channels.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core result about AutoGater's performance using light-scatter channels with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stain-free method outlined for identifying viable cells improves the efficiency of flow cytometry analysis by eliminating the need for fluorescent stains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stain-free method outlined for identifying viable cells can improve the efficiency of flow cytometry data analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the claim slightly with "can improve," clarifying the potential benefit and tempering confidence compared with the preprint's stronger wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoGater harmonizes different measurements of dead cells such as Sytox and CFUs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoGater harmonizes different measurements of dead cells such as Sytox and CFUs.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that AutoGater harmonizes different dead-cell measurements with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional neural network-based algorithm can fast upscale low-resolution or sparsely sampled images for 3D reconstruction and statistical analysis of vascular network structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network-based algorithm can fast upscale low-resolution or sparsely sampled images for 3D reconstruction and statistical analysis of vascular network structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about the CNN-based upscaling algorithm with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of semi-synthetic training data can replace the process of acquiring low- and high-resolution training pairs without compromising vectorization outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of semi-synthetic training data can replace the process of acquiring low- and high-resolution training pairs without compromising vectorization outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about semi-synthetic training data as a substitute for paired acquisitions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method generalizes across imaging depths, disease states, and other differences in neurovasculature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method generalizes across imaging depths, disease states, and other differences in neurovasculature.
alignments.rationale: The generalization claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pre-trained models and lightweight architecture can reduce MPM imaging time by up to fourfold without changes in underlying hardware.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pretrained models and lightweight architecture can reduce MPM imaging time by up to fourfold without changes in underlying hardware.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same quantitative claim about imaging time reduction and conditions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AIMLinker is a deep neural network proposed for the rapid design and generation of drug-like PROTACs analogs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AIMLinker is a deep neural network that can rapidly design and generate meaningful drug-like proteolysis targeting chimeras (PROTACs) analogs.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same contribution claim about AIMLinker enabling rapid design of PROTAC analogs with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated novel PROTACs molecules possess similar structural information with superior binding affinity to the binding pockets compared to existing CRBN-dBET6-BRD4 ternary complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated novel PROTACs possess similar structural information with superior binding affinity to the binding pockets compared to existing CRBN-dBET6-BRD4 ternary complexes.
alignments.rationale: The result about generated PROTACs having similar structure and superior binding affinity is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AIMLinker effectively designs compounds for PROTACs molecules with better chemical properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology of leveraging AIMLinker demonstrates effectiveness in designing novel compounds for PROTACs with better chemical properties compared to the dBET6 crystal pose.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the baseline (dBET6 crystal pose) used for comparison, clarifying and narrowing the preprint's broader claim about improved chemical properties while maintaining overall effectiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural network (CNN) models applied to three-dimensional MRI data can perform completely data-driven and automatic feature extraction for Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural network (CNN) models can be applied to three-dimensional MRI data for automatic feature extraction in Alzheimer's disease research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent scope and certainty, so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The image-derived features from CNN models are used as endophenotypes in Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS) to identify associated genetic variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Image-derived features from CNN models can be used as endophenotypes in genome-wide association studies (GWASs) to identify genetic variants associated with Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same use of image-derived CNN features as endophenotypes for GWAS to identify AD-associated variants with comparable phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several associated SNPs identified in the study have been previously shown to be related to neurodegenerative and mental disorders such as Alzheimer's disease, depression, and schizophrenia. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of this method to ADNI data identified several associated SNPs related to neurodegenerative and mental disorders, including Alzheimer's disease, depression, and schizophrenia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same finding (SNPs related to AD, depression, schizophrenia) albeit with slightly stronger certainty and explicit ADNI dataset mention; content remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ImageJ was error-prone in measuring seed counts due to undercounting seeds that were touching each other. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The segmentation-based method for measuring seed counts in Arabidopsis thaliana is error-prone, with a correlation of r² = 0.849. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a quantitative performance measure and frames the issue as a segmentation-based method problem; this clarifies and quantifies the preprint observation without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Faster R-CNN yielded near perfect seed counts and highly accurate fruit counts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The object detection-based algorithm provides near perfect seed counts with a correlation of r² = 0.9996 and highly accurate fruit counts with a correlation of r² = 0.980. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supplies exact performance metrics for the object-detection approach (Faster R-CNN), thereby clarifying and quantifying the preprint claim while preserving its meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By examining seed counts, we were able to reveal fitness effects for genes that were previously reported to have no or condition-specific loss-of-function phenotypes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comparing seed counts for wild-type and 12 mutant lines revealed fitness effects for three genes, while fruit counts revealed the same effects for two genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports specific numbers of genes with detected fitness effects but drops the explicit claim about overturning prior 'no or condition-specific' phenotype reports, narrowing and revising the original interpretation and reducing its broader claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study provides models to facilitate the investigation of Arabidopsis fitness traits. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides analysis pipelines and models to facilitate the investigation of Arabidopsis fitness traits. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the contribution description by adding 'analysis pipelines' but essentially confirms the availability of models to facilitate fitness-trait investigation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the importance of examining fitness traits in the study of gene functions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the importance of examining fitness traits when studying gene functions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMP is a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based model that detects whether a given motif in a read is methylated by utilizing information from both Nanopore signals and basecalling errors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMP is a convolutional neural network-based model that detects whether a given motif in a read is methylated by utilizing information from both Nanopore signals and basecalling errors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution description verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMP introduces a threshold-free position modification calling model that is sensitive to sites methylated at low frequency across cells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMP introduces a threshold-free position modification calling model that is sensitive to sites methylated at low frequency across cells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this methodological contribution exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMP outperforms current approaches in read-based and position-based methylation detection across sites methylated at different frequencies in E. coli, human, and pUC19 datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMP outperforms current approaches in read-based and position-based methylation detection across sites methylated at different frequencies in Escherichia coli, human, and pUC19 datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is preserved with identical scope and datasets (spelled out), so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Approximately 72% of Transcription Start Sites (TSSs) could not be assigned to a specific gene and initiate at unconventional regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Approximately 72% of transcription start sites (TSSs) could not be assigned to a specific gene and initiate at unconventional regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative result with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A significant fraction of CAGE peaks initiate at microsatellites, also known as short tandem repeats (STRs), across all species studied. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A significant fraction of CAGE peaks initiate at microsatellites, also known as short tandem repeats (STRs), across all species studied. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this finding verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sequence-based deep learning models were trained to predict CAGE signal at STRs with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sequence-based deep learning models can predict CAGE signal at STRs with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same methodological result (prediction of CAGE signal at STRs) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genetic variants linked to human diseases affect STR-associated transcription and correspond to key positions identified by the models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genetic variants linked to human diseases are preferentially found at STRs with high transcription initiation levels. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim to an association (variants preferentially found at highly transcribed STRs) rather than asserting variants affect transcription or correspond to model-identified key positions, thus narrowing and tempering the causal language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results extend the repertoire of non-coding transcription associated with DNA tandem repeats and complexify STR polymorphism. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results extend the repertoire of non-coding transcription associated with DNA tandem repeats and complexify STR polymorphism. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this interpretive conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model was trained to predict mean ribosome load (MRL) directly from 5’UTR sequence with a high degree of accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model was trained to predict mean ribosome load (MRL) directly from 5'UTR sequence with a high degree of accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.rationale: The contribution is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model shows state-of-the-art performance on fixed length randomized sequences and better generalization performance on longer sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model shows state-of-the-art performance on fixed length randomized sequences and better generalization performance on longer sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this performance claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variant interpretation is demonstrated on a 5’UTR variant of the gene HBB associated with beta-thalassemia. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model could help pinpoint pathogenic genetic variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the explicit claim about pinpointing pathogenic variants; instead it adds a different concluding claim about broader applications of frame pooling, so this specific preprint claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) predicts protein functions and identifies functionally important residues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Class activation mapping allows function predictions at an unprecedented resolution, enabling site-specific annotations at the residue-level in an automated manner. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claim about identifying functionally important residues maps best to the publication's residue-level, automated class activation mapping claim; the publication frames this with stronger language ('unprecedented resolution'), amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates significant de-noising capability, with only a minor drop in performance when using structure predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepFRI demonstrates significant de-noising capability, with only a minor drop in performance when experimental structures are replaced by protein models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same de-noising result with essentially the same conditions and magnitude of effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our GCN architecture predicts functions more accurately than Convolutional Neural Networks trained on sequence data alone and previous competing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepFRI outperforms current leading methods and sequence-based Convolutional Neural Networks in predicting protein functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This is a direct match: both statements claim superior function-prediction performance relative to CNNs and other leading methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can robustly predict functions of proteins with ≤ 40% sequence identity to the training set. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim specifically restates or quantifies robustness at ≤40% sequence identity, so this specific claim appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method advances site-specific function prediction by automatically identifying structural regions at the residue-level for each function prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Class activation mapping allows function predictions at an unprecedented resolution, enabling site-specific annotations at the residue-level in an automated manner. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands and strengthens the preprint's site-specific/residue-level claim, emphasizing automation and higher resolution, which clarifies and amplifies the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our methods for critiquing deep learning models demonstrate their application for protein family classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods for critiquing deep learning models can enhance understanding of model predictions in protein family classification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the application of the critiquing methods to protein family classification with equivalent intent and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tools we developed expose that deep models may perform classification for biologically-relevant reasons. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep models may perform classification for biologically relevant reasons, but their behavior varies significantly with different network architectures and parameter initializations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core point that models can rely on biologically relevant features, adding discussion of variability; the biological-relevance claim itself is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The behavior of deep models varies considerably across choice of network architecture and parameter initialization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep models may perform classification for biologically relevant reasons, but their behavior varies significantly with different network architectures and parameter initializations. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach taken in our techniques generalizes to a broad set of scientific contexts where model interpretability is essential. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RL preserves biological plausibility within the optimized networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RL preserves biological plausibility within the optimized networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No substantive change in claim content or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights RL’s significant potential as an instrumental tool in computational biology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights RL’s significant potential as an instrumental tool in computational biology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same concluding claim with unchanged scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Challenges were encountered in achieving persistent oscillations and matching traditional algorithms’ computational speed. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Challenges were encountered in achieving persistent oscillations and matching traditional algorithms’ computational speed. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Limitation statement preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research establishes RL as a promising strategy for manipulating and designing intricate behaviors in biological networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research establishes RL as a promising strategy for manipulating and designing intricate behaviors in biological networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion retained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonlinear reinforcement learning incorporating divisive normalization introduces an intrinsic and tunable asymmetry in prediction error coding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonlinear reinforcement learning incorporating divisive normalization introduces an intrinsic and tunable asymmetry in prediction error coding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The asymmetry in prediction error coding explains empirical variability in risk preferences typically attributed to asymmetric learning rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The asymmetry in prediction error coding explains empirical variability in risk preferences typically attributed to asymmetric learning rates. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diversity in asymmetries provides a computational mechanism for theories of distributional reinforcement learning, allowing the brain to learn the full probability distribution of future rewards. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diversity in asymmetries provides a computational mechanism for theories of distributional reinforcement learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the stronger neuroscientific claim about learning full reward distributions and downgrades certainty/role, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings argue for the incorporation of biologically valid value functions in computational models of learning and decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings argue for the incorporation of biologically valid value functions in computational models of learning and decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion retained with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sounds of diverse quality, but equal intensity and perceptual detectability, can recruit different levels of population activity in mouse auditory cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific empirical claim about diverse-quality sounds recruiting different population activity is present in the preprint but not represented as a standalone claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The degree of cortical recruitment matches the salience parameter of a reinforcement learning model used to analyze learning speed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cortical recruitment matches the salience parameter of a reinforcement learning model used to analyze learning speed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is carried over verbatim (minor wording) and retains meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cortical recruitment causally determines association or overshadowing of the stimulus components. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cortical recruitment causally determines association or overshadowing of the stimulus components. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Directly preserved between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cortical recruitment underlies major aspects of stimulus salience during reinforcement learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cortical recruitment underlies major aspects of stimulus salience during reinforcement learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion maintained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural architectures significantly outperform both shallow machine learning and standard, homology-based algorithms in predicting whether a virus can infect humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural architectures significantly outperform both shallow machine learning and standard, homology-based algorithms in predicting whether a virus can infect humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The error rates in viral host prediction are cut in half using the proposed deep learning methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The error rates in viral host prediction are cut in half using the proposed deep learning methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Quantitative performance claim retained unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nucleotide-resolution maps of learned associations between pathogen genomes and the infectious phenotype can be used to detect regions of interest in novel agents. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nucleotide-resolution maps of learned associations can be used to detect regions of interest in novel agents, such as SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim but rephrases it and adds a concrete example (SARS-CoV-2); overall meaning preserved though slightly reworded.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interpretability tools developed can be applied to models beyond the host prediction task. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interpretability tools developed can be applied to models beyond the host prediction task. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim about tool generalizability is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All methods presented are implemented as easy-to-install packages for analyzing NGS datasets without requiring deep learning skills. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This implementation/distribution claim from the preprint does not appear among the publication claims provided and thus is treated as removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMol is an Automated ML (AutoML) tool that automates critical steps of the ML pipeline in computational chemistry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMol automates critical steps of the machine learning pipeline for computational chemistry.
alignments.rationale: Wording and scope are effectively identical between preprint and publication, expressing the same contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMol rapidly and automatically identifies the most effective data representation, pre-processing methods, and model configurations for molecular property/activity prediction problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmarking on diverse datasets demonstrated DeepMol's ability to deliver optimized pipelines and superior performance across various molecular machine-learning tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific automated selection of representations/preprocessing/configs as delivering optimized pipelines across tasks, which clarifies and broadens the original operational claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On 22 benchmark datasets, DeepMol obtained competitive pipelines compared to those requiring time-consuming feature engineering and model design processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: On 22 benchmark datasets, DeepMol obtained competitive pipelines compared with those requiring time-consuming feature engineering.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same benchmark result but with higher stated certainty, so the substance is unchanged while confidence is stronger.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMol is one of the first AutoML tools specifically developed for the computational chemistry domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert novelty as being "one of the first"; no matching claim appears in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMol establishes itself as the pioneering state-of-the-art tool in the field of computational chemistry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmarking on diverse datasets demonstrated DeepMol's ability to deliver optimized pipelines and superior performance across various molecular machine-learning tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports strong performance but stops short of an explicit "pioneering state-of-the-art" claim, softening the original assertive conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The genomic language model (gLM) learns contextualized protein embeddings that capture both genomic context and protein sequence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: gLM learns contextualized protein embeddings that capture the genomic context as well as the protein sequence itself.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical result with near-identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: gLM encodes biologically meaningful and functionally relevant information, such as enzymatic function and taxonomy.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state the specific functional/taxonomic encodings claimed in the preprint, so this specific result is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of attention patterns indicates that gLM is learning co-regulated functional modules, such as operons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: gLM is learning co-regulated functional modules, such as operons, as demonstrated by the analysis of attention patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same claim but with higher stated certainty, preserving the content while amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: gLM’s unsupervised deep learning approach is effective in encoding functional semantics and regulatory syntax of genes in their genomic contexts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: gLM’s unsupervised deep learning of the metagenomic corpus is an effective approach to encode functional semantics and regulatory syntax of genes.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conclusion with a focus on the metagenomic corpus and uses a more cautious certainty level, clarifying scope and tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of transfer learning using embeddings from protein language models allows for effective prediction of protein thermostability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method TemStaPro predicts protein thermostability using embeddings generated by protein language models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological result using equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TemStaPro, the developed prediction method, was trained and validated using over one million sequences from organisms with annotated growth temperatures.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the explicit training/validation dataset size and provenance claim present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictions made by TemStaPro indicated sharp differences in thermostability among groups of CRISPR-Cas Class II effector proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictions from TemStaPro indicated sharp differences among groups of CRISPR-Cas Class II effector proteins in terms of thermostability.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific prediction result with higher stated certainty than the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions of TemStaPro were largely consistent with previously published and newly obtained experimental data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictions made by TemStaPro were largely consistent with previously published and newly obtained experimental data.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same consistency claim but lists it with slightly lower certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHINE achieved better prediction performance than existing methods for both deletion and insertion variants in two test datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHINE achieved better prediction performance than existing methods for both deletion and insertion variants in two test datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the benchmarking result verbatim, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate pathogenicity predictions are important in genetic studies of human diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate this general background/importance claim; it focuses on methodological and technical contributions instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHINE uses pre-trained protein language models to construct a latent representation of an indel and its protein context.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHINE uses pretrained protein language models to construct a latent representation of an indel and its protein context.
alignments.rationale: This methodological contribution is presented the same way in the publication as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing methods largely use manually encoded features including conservation, protein structure and function, and allele frequency.
alignments.rationale: The publication instead emphasizes data limitations and modeling advances; it does not reiterate the specific characterization of existing methods' features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work suggests that unsupervised protein language models can provide valuable information about proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advances in deep learning modeling of protein sequences and structures provide an opportunity to improve the representation of salient features based on large numbers of protein sequences.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the conclusion in a broader context of deep learning advances and with higher certainty, clarifying and strengthening the original modest claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Machine Learning-guided Antigenic Evolution Prediction (MLAEP) can infer the order of SARS-COV-2 variants along antigenic evolutionary trajectories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MLAEP accurately infers variant order along antigenic evolutionary trajectories, correlating with corresponding sampling time.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds emphasis on accuracy and correlation with sampling time, providing a clarified and slightly more specific framing of the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel mutations predicted by MLAEP are found in immunocompromised COVID patients and newly emerging variants, such as XBB1.5.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MLAEP identified novel mutations in immunocompromised COVID-19 patients and emerging variants like XBB1.5.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this observation with stronger stated certainty, preserving the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions of MLAEP were validated by in vitro neutralizing antibody binding assays, which demonstrated increased immune evasion ability of model-generated variants compared to controls.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicted variants exhibited enhanced immune evasion as validated through in vitro neutralizing antibody binding assays.
alignments.rationale: The validation result is restated with equivalent meaning and certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MLAEP enables profiling existing variants and forecasting prospective antigenic variants, potentially guiding vaccine development and preparedness against future variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MLAEP aids in vaccine development and enhances preparedness against future SARS-CoV-2 variants.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same applied-consequence claim but states it more directly in terms of aiding vaccine development and preparedness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modeling schema formulated in this study overcomes the limitations of current methods through applications of Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modeling schema formulated in this study overcomes the limitations of current methods through applications of artificial intelligence machine learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations using the IKs structure-function model reproduced experimentally recorded saturation of gating charge displacement at positive membrane voltages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations using the IKs structure-function model reproduced experimentally recorded saturation of gating-charge displacement at positive membrane voltages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the identical simulation result and language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pore energy profile determines ion-channel subconductance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pore energy profile determines ion channel subconductance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same conclusion in the publication with only minor punctuation/formatting differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interactions with KCNE1 result in two distinct voltage sensor movements, causing gating charge saturation at positive membrane voltages and current activation delay. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interactions with KCNE1 result in two distinct voltage sensor movements, causing gating-charge saturation at positive membrane voltages and current activation delay. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same mechanistic conclusion with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new modeling approach is applicable to atomistic scale studies of other proteins on timescales of physiological function. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new modeling approach is applicable to atomistic scale studies of other proteins on timescales of physiological function. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same generalizability claim with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Digital holographic microscopy combined with deep learning can classify key CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Digital holographic microscopy combined with deep learning can classify key CD4 + and CD8 + T cell subsets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same classification result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification throughput rate achieved is 78,000 cells per second with an accuracy of 76.2%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification throughput rate achieved is 78,000 cells per second with an accuracy of 76.2%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported throughput and accuracy are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This throughput rate is 100 times faster than previous studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This throughput rate is 100 times faster than previous studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative throughput claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method proves to be an effective replacement for traditional labelling methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method proves to be an effective replacement for traditional labelling methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding recommendation about replacing labeling methods is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Indirect estimation of oxygen consumption using neural networks combined with motion parameters and heart rate measurements yields reasonably accurate results for intra-subject estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Indirect estimation of oxygen consumption using neural networks combined with motion features and heart rate measurements yields reasonably accurate results for intra-subject estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
